# Scout Session Transcript — session_55

❯ Read /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/CLAUDE.md and /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/SCOUT_PLAN.md, then continue from the Session 54 Fix Sprint plan in CLAUDE.md CURRENT STATE.

 Session 53 was a Fire Drill Audit that discovered 5 silent-failure bugs in Scout's scanner + research pipeline. Only 1 code fix shipped (F2 max_tokens cap, commit 7c345a07). The other 5 bugs are parked in
 project memories (project_f4_funding_scanner_broken.md, project_f5_csta_scanner_low_yield.md, project_f2_column_layout_corruption.md, project_f8_diocesan_research_playbook.md,

 project_research_cross_contamination.md) — read those before starting.

 Session 54 is a DEDICATED FIX SPRINT. No new scanners, no new strategies, no new features. Rules:

 1. Enter plan mode BEFORE touching any code. Session 53 bypassed plan mode for Stage 4 execution and lost control. Don't repeat.

 2. Build a local diagnostic harness that can replay Serper results + exercise add_district without Railway redeploys. Every fix should be validatable locally before deploy.

 3. Work the priority order from CLAUDE.md CURRENT STATE: BUG 3 (column layout) → BUG 5 (research cross-contamination) → BUG 4 (diocesan playbook) → BUG 1 (F4 queries) → BUG 2 (F5 strategic decision) → Stage

 6-8 Session 52 carryover.

 4. BUG 3 is priority 1 because it blocks trust in ANY scanner that calls add_district. Without BUG 3 fixed, running Stage 6 (charter CMOs + CTE centers) could add 122 more scrambled rows.

 5. BUG 3 requires finding the secondary writer or race condition. SAME add_district call produced canonical layout for F5/F8 and scrambled layout for F2 in the same session window. Git archaeology found no

 version of add_district that writes the scrambled layout. Hypothesis: Railway rolling deploy temporal inconsistency OR hidden secondary writer. Needs audit.

 Before starting the first fix, also:

 - Check Railway status and confirm commits 7c345a07, 89fa279, 3431323, 3a39334 are all live

 - Pittsburgh Public Schools (PA) and Archdiocese of Chicago Schools (IL) are sitting in the Prospecting Queue as canonical rows waiting for manual review/approval. Archdiocese has a completed Cold

 Google Doc from Session 53. Don't touch either until I say so.

 - Tulsa PS draft is scheduled to send Tuesday 8:05 AM. If that's already happened by the time you read this, confirm it landed.

 Start by entering plan mode and sketching the BUG 3 investigation approach. Don't write any code until I've approved the plan.

 ⏺ Key findings before exiting plan mode:

 - Agent 1's proximity bug is real but separate. proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415-435) writes 19 cols, missing Signal ID. That's a latent off-by-one, but its layout
 (Strategy at col 9) does NOT match the Lackland fingerprint (Strategy at col 3). Not the BUG 3 root cause — but fixing it is in Phase 6.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Agent 2's "F8 missing kwargs" theory is backwards. F8 Archdiocese was confirmed canonical by direct sheet read in Session 53; F2 Lackland was the scrambled one. Agent 2 misread the attribution. Ruled out.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 migration runs.

 systematically.

 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 Session 54 — Fix Sprint: BUG 3 Investigation Plan

 Context

 Session 53 was a Fire Drill Audit that uncovered 5 silent-failure bugs in Scout's scanner + research pipeline. Session 54 is a dedicated fix sprint — no new scanners, no new strategies, no new features. This

 plan covers BUG 3 (column layout corruption) only, which is the highest-priority blocker because it corrupts every scanner that calls add_district and is already polluting the Prospecting Queue at scale (1912

 pre-existing scrambled rows found in Session 53, plus 8 new rows from tonight's F2 run).

 Why BUG 3 first: Running Stages 6-8 carryover (F6 charter CMOs + F7 CTE centers + F9 compliance pilot) without a fix could add 122+ more scrambled rows. Until we find the ghost writer, we cannot trust any

 scanner that touches the queue. Fixing this unblocks everything else in the sprint.

 What Session 53 proved and did not prove:

 - ✅ Canonical add_district in tools/district_prospector.py:2482-2564 writes a 20-column row with Strategy at col 9. Verified by local sentinel-row test.

 - ✅ F5 ( scan_csta_chapters ) and F8 ( queue_private_school_networks ) produced canonical rows in the same session window as F2 (Pittsburgh PS at 01:14 UTC, Archdiocese at 01:32 UTC, Lackland et al at 00:51 UTC).

 - ❌ Unsolved: What code path produced the Lackland scrambled layout? No writer in the current checkout matches the pattern: filled cols 1-7 (state, name, strategy, priority, source, enrollment, notes), empty

 8-12, filled 13-15 (name_key, status="pending", date_added), empty 16-19, filled 20 (signal_id).

 - ❌ Unsolved: Why 1912 rows with the same scrambled layout already existed in the queue (some dating to 2026-03-20 with "winback" strategy), meaning this writer has been running for weeks.

 Investigation plan — phased, stop-and-report at each checkpoint

 Phase 0 — Pre-flight (read-only, ~15 min)

 - Confirm Railway deployment: commits 7c345a07 , 89fa279 , 3431323 , 3a39334 are live and singular (no old pods still serving traffic). Use Railway API via tools/railway_api.py or direct CLI (note: memory says

 Vercel CLI isn't installed — check whether Railway CLI is).

 - Do not touch Pittsburgh PS (PA, pending, csta_partnership) or Archdiocese of Chicago (IL, pending, private_school_network) queue rows. Per-Steven hold.

 - Check whether Tulsa PS draft sent Tuesday 8:05 AM (if current date ≥ that time); if not yet, leave alone.

 Phase 1 — Local diagnostic harness (prerequisite for ALL fixes)

 Why first: Every other fix in this sprint depends on being able to replay scanner output locally without redeploying Railway. Session 53's F2 diagnostic only worked because Steven built an ad-hoc IL replay.

 Formalize that.

 Build tools/diagnostics/queue_writer_harness.py (or similar) that can:

 1. Import district_prospector and call add_district with arbitrary kwargs against a SANDBOX tab (not TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE ). Sandbox tab name: Prospecting Queue SANDBOX or reuse an existing test tab.

 2. Read every row written and fingerprint it by layout pattern (see Phase 2).

 3. Replay a serialized Serper result set + Claude extraction output through scan_competitor_displacement 's post-extract loop (lines ~3748-3764 in tools/signal_processor.py ) without hitting live APIs.

 4. Produce a before/after diff against the sandbox tab.

 Critical files to reuse, not rebuild:

 - tools/district_prospector.py:426 — _write_rows (the core writer)

 - tools/district_prospector.py:2482 — add_district signature + body

 - tools/signal_processor.py:3748-3764 — F2 auto-queue loop (for replay)

 Harness must be runnable via python -m tools.diagnostics.queue_writer_harness locally with Google credentials, without Railway deploy.

 Phase 2 — Fingerprint audit of live queue

 Write a read-only script that reads every row of TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE and classifies each by layout signature:

 - Canonical (20-col): strategy at index 8, status at index 10, signal_id at index 18, notes at index 19

 - Legacy-19 (proximity, missing Signal ID): strategy at index 8, notes at index 18, no col 20

 - Lackland-scrambled: strategy at index 2 (col 3), priority at index 3, name_key at index 12, status at index 13, signal_id at index 19

 - Legacy-16 (old migration target): strategy at index 5 (col 6)

 - Unknown/other

 Output: row count per fingerprint, date-added range per fingerprint, strategy distribution per fingerprint, 5 sample rows per fingerprint.

 Use this to confirm:

 - Exactly which layouts are in the wild (expected: canonical, Lackland-scrambled, legacy-19 from proximity, plus possibly one or more unknowns)

 - Whether the 1912 pre-existing "status=school/district" rows match Lackland-scrambled or a different fingerprint

 - Whether the Lackland-scrambled rows ever contain strategies that only existed post-Session-52 (e.g., csta_partnership , charter_cmo ) — that would rule out stale-Railway-worker theory

 Phase 3 — Hunt the ghost writer

 Session 53's audit verified these writers are NOT the culprit:

 - district_prospector.add_district + _write_rows (canonical)

 - proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (writes legacy-19, NOT Lackland layout)

 - migrate_prospect_columns / cleanup_prospect_queue / reprioritize_pending (handle reads, can't create Lackland layout)

 - _update_status (writes to specific row_num, uses A:S read — LATENT BUG: truncates col 20 Notes, but not the Lackland bug)

 - No scripts in scripts/ append to Prospecting Queue (they only patchUpdate specific cells)

 - No direct writes in compliance_gap_scanner.py , charter_prospector.py , cte_prospector.py , private_schools.py

 1. Deleted code: git log -p --all --diff-filter=D -- tools/district_prospector.py and same for signal_processor.py , sheets_writer.py , csv_importer.py , lead_importer.py . Look for any historical version that

 built rows in [state, name, strategy, priority, source, enrollment, notes] order. If we find it, check whether any currently-imported function still routes through that code path.

 2. CSV import path: /import_leads , /import_contacts , or /import_clear accidentally writing to Prospecting Queue instead of Leads/Contacts. Check tools/lead_importer.py and tools/csv_importer.py auto-detect

 logic (headers → routing). Verify no CSV column mapping could cause queue rows to be written with [state, name, strategy, ...] ordering.

 3. Shared worker + unbounded kwargs: Re-check whether **kwargs forwarding in add_district could ever produce a non-20-element row. The code pops est_enrollment / school_count / total_licenses and forwards the

 rest to _calculate_priority . If _calculate_priority mutates kwargs or has a side-effect that affects row construction — audit that function.

 4. Stale Railway pod (last resort): Memory already suggests this. Confirm by checking Railway deployment logs for rolling-deploy overlap on 2026-04-10 00:48-00:52 UTC window. If confirmed, the fix is

 operational (force restart + single-pod guarantee) not code — still need the repair script for historical damage.

 5. Google Sheets API edge case: _write_rows uses range=!A2 + insert Data Option=INSERT_ROWS . Verify this cannot fragment a single 20-element row across columns under any edge case (e.g., concurrent header

 rewrite in _ensure_tab racing the append).

 Phase 4 — Capture live evidence on next F2 run

 Before any fix ships, add temporary debug logging to _write_rows that logs the full rows payload + call stack on every call. Trigger F2 manually once with ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN flipped on, spot-check whether

 the scrambled output still happens, and if so — the debug log will reveal the caller. Revert the logging commit after evidence is captured.

 Kill switch status check before Phase 4:

 - ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN (F4): currently False per commit 3431323 — leave disabled

 - ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN (F5): currently False per commit 3431323 — leave disabled

 - ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN (F2): need to verify state; probably still True because it was just fixed in 7c345a07 . If True, consider flipping False during Phase 1-3 and only re-enabling for Phase 4 controlled

 test.

 Phase 5 — Fix + one-shot repair script

 Only after Phases 1-4 identify the writer:

 1. Fix the writer at its source (may be as simple as a column-order correction, or as disruptive as removing a dead code path).

 2. Write scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py that:

 - Reads all rows from TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE

 - Classifies by fingerprint (reuse Phase 2 classifier)

 - For each scrambled row, maps known-position values back to canonical positions using the layout map from the memory (col 3 → col 9 for strategy, col 4 → col 12 for priority, col 5 → col 10 for source, col

 6 → col 16 for enrollment, col 7 → col 20 for notes, col 13 → col 8 for name_key, col 14 → col 11 for status, col 15 → col 13 for date_added, col 20 → col 19 for signal_id)

 - Writes dry-run diff to stdout first (print N scrambled rows and their proposed canonical rewrite)

 - Only after human approval, clear + rewrite sheet with migrated rows using the existing migrate_prospect_columns clear-and-update pattern (lines 228-239)

 3. Extend migrate_prospect_columns in tools/district_prospector.py:173 to recognize the Lackland fingerprint AND add ALL Session 49+ strategies to _KNOWN_STRATEGIES (currently missing: competitor_displacement ,

 csta_partnership , charter_cmo , cte_center , private_school_network , compliance_gap , intra_district , cs_funding_recipient , homeschool_coop , proximity , esa_cluster ).

 4. Run repair script on live sheet. Expect: 8 F2 rows + 1912 pre-existing scrambled rows repaired to canonical.

 5. Spot-check via /prospect_all that the 8 F2 rows now surface, and via direct sheet read that the 1912 historical rows now have Status="pending" (or whatever their migrated state should be — TBD based on what

 col 14 actually held).

 Phase 6 — Also fix the latent bugs found during investigation

 These are non-blocking but should be fixed in the same sprint since they touch the same files:

 - proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects ( tools/proximity_engine.py:415-435 ): row has 19 elements — missing Signal ID at canonical col 19. Insert "" between str(d.get("enrollment", "")) (col 16) and note

 (currently col 19, should be col 20). Three empty strings between enrollment/school_count/licenses and notes currently — add Signal ID slot. This explains Agent 1's finding.

 - _update_status ( tools/district_prospector.py:440-471 ): reads A:S (19 cols), writes back padded[:len(headers)] . Silently destroys Notes column (col 20) on every status update. Change range to A:T (or

 A:{last_col_letter} ) and preserve 20-col output.

 Phase 7 — Verification before declaring BUG 3 fixed

 - Local harness run (Phase 1) produces canonical rows for F2, F5, F8, F6, F7, F9 simulated inputs

 - Fingerprint audit (Phase 2) run against live sheet shows 0 Lackland-scrambled rows remaining

 - /prospect_all Telegram command returns the 8 F2 leads (Lackland TX, Mansfield TX, Naperville 203 IL, etc.) as pending rows

 - F2 manual run (if re-enabled) writes a single sentinel row that fingerprints canonical

 - Evidence logged to commit message of the fix

 Critical files (reference, not edit list)

 - tools/district_prospector.py — add_district (2482), _write_rows (426), migrate_prospect_columns (173), _update_status (440), _ensure_tab (144)

 - tools/signal_processor.py — F2 auto-queue block (3748-3764), F5 (4757-4780), F4 (3382-3396)

 - tools/proximity_engine.py — add_proximity_prospects (394-441)

 - tools/private_schools.py:234-242 — F8 queue call

 - tools/charter_prospector.py + tools/cte_prospector.py — F6/F7 queue calls

 - agent/main.py:2996 — /prospect_add direct dispatch path

 - scripts/ — read-only audit scripts, reference for how to connect to sheets without the main bot

 Out of scope for this plan

 - BUG 1 (F4 query redesign) — Priority 4 in sprint

 - BUG 2 (F5 strategic decision) — Priority 5, needs Steven's strategic answer first

 - BUG 4 (F8 diocesan playbook) — Priority 3

 - BUG 5 (research cross-contamination) — Priority 2, separate plan

 - Stages 6-8 Session 52 carryover — Priority 6

 - Any UI / Telegram / sequence changes

 Verification for this plan itself

 1. Phase order is right (0 → 7)

 2. Local harness shape is acceptable (or prefers a different approach — e.g., temporary sandbox tab vs live tab rollback)

 3. Whether to flip F2 kill switch to False during Phases 1-3

 4. Whether we can touch Railway at all during the investigation or if we're forbidden to deploy until Phase 5 fix lands

 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌

 ❯ T ell Claude what to change

 Refine this plan. Your job is to pressure-test it honestly — keep what's
 genuinely strong, rebuild what isn't, and hand back something I can execute
 with full confidence.
 Act as a ruthless, senior-level technical reviewer and architect. Be direct,

 specific, and opinionated. No hedging, no diplomacy, no soft language around

 real problems. If something is wrong, say it's wrong and say why. But the

 inverse also holds: if something is right, leave it alone. Do not rewrite solid

 work just to look thorough — churn for its own sake is its own kind of

 failure. The bar for changing something is that the new version is meaningfully

 better, not merely different.

 happen because the reviewer started reacting before they finished

 understanding.

 1. Challenge the foundation. Is the overall approach actually right, or is it

 just the first thing that came to mind? Interrogate the architecture, the

 framing, and the core assumptions before touching details. If there's a

 fundamentally better angle — different architecture, different sequencing,

 different tradeoffs, a smaller problem hiding inside the big one — say so and

 restructure around it. Do not anchor to your first answer because you already

 committed to it on the page.

 2. Stress-test every layer as if you're executing it tomorrow. Walk the plan

 end to end and ask: could I actually sit down and build this without hitting a

 wall? Find the hidden assumptions, the vague steps, the dependency tangles, the

 places where things will quietly break at the edges, the spots where "it'll

 just work" is doing real load-bearing work. For every problem: name it, explain

 why it happens, explain what it costs, and give the fix. Diagnosis without

 prescription is useless.

 3. Pressure-test every technical decision against the best available knowledge.

 Architecture, tooling, patterns, data modeling, sequencing — for each one, ask

 whether it's genuinely the strongest option or just the most familiar. Weigh

 it against what actually holds up in production, not what's popular in

 tutorials or trending this quarter. Where real tradeoffs exist between

 competing approaches, surface them honestly and take a clear position on which

 one I should pick and why. No "it depends" cop-outs.

 4. Optimize for the highest-quality result, reached as fast and efficiently as

 possible. Every step should be pulling its weight toward a correct, durable,

 high-quality outcome — not just toward motion. Ruthlessly cut anything that

 adds time without adding quality, and ruthlessly add anything that protects

 quality even if it costs a little time upfront. Sequence the work so that the

 decisions and pieces with the highest leverage on the final result come first,

 so that mistakes get caught as early and cheaply as possible, and so that

 nothing downstream has to be redone because something upstream was rushed.

 Where two paths exist, pick the one that gets to the correct answer with the

 least wasted motion — and say why. Speed comes from not doing the wrong work,

 not from skipping the right work.

 5. Cut the fat, keep the muscle. Depth is welcome. Redundancy, vagueness, and

 ceremony are not. Every element earns its place or it goes. If a step would

 make me stop and ask "what does this actually mean in practice," either make it

 concrete or delete it. If two steps do the same work, collapse them. If a

 section exists because it sounds thorough rather than because it changes the

 outcome, kill it.

 6. Fill the gaps a senior engineer would catch in a design review. What's

 missing that looks minor now and will hurt later? Edge cases, error handling,

 failure modes, prerequisites, migration paths, ordering constraints, and the

 easy-to-punt decisions that get expensive to reverse. Surface them now, while

 they're cheap.

 7. Rewrite the full plan from scratch. Do not patch the original — rebuild it

 with everything above baked in. Make it comprehensive, precise, and immediately

 actionable. Preserve the parts of the original that were already strong —

 rebuilding does not mean discarding what worked. The goal is the best possible

 plan, not the most rewritten one. Then, at the end, list the most important

 changes you made and explain the reasoning behind each one so I can judge

 whether your thinking holds up.

 Take your time. Think deeply. This is the version I'm going to execute from —

 make it the best one you're capable of producing.

 ⏺ Bash (git log --all --o e ine --since="2026-03-15" --until="2026-04-10")

 3a39334 docs: SCOUT_HISTORY Session 53 Fire Drill Audit entry

 ⎿  Loaded tools/CLAUDE.md

 ⏺ I have enough context to write the plan. Let me also quickly look at the sheets_writer paths and check scripts/ for any direct queue writers.

 ⏺ Updated plan
 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⏺ Key findings before exiting plan mode:

 - Agent 1's proximity bug is real but separate. proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415-435) writes 19 cols, missing Signal ID. That's a latent off-by-one, but its layout
 (Strategy at col 9) does NOT match the Lackland fingerprint (Strategy at col 3). Not the BUG 3 root cause — but fixing it is in Phase 6.
 - Agent 2's "F8 missing kwargs" theory is backwards. F8 Archdiocese was confirmed canonical by direct sheet read in Session 53; F2 Lackland was the scrambled one. Agent 2 misread the attribution. Ruled out.
 - _update_status (district_prospector.py:440-471) reads A:S (19 cols) and silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update — another latent bug. In Phase 6.
 migrate_prospect_columns (district_prospec or.py:173) only knows 4 legacy strategies in _KNOWN_STRATEGIES — missing ALL 10 Session 49+ strategies. Ghost writer's rows would be kept as-is even if the
 mig ion runs.
 Th Lackland fingerprint matches NO current code path. Either a deleted code path is sti l somehow rout d to, a CSV-imp rt path is miswriting, or there's a stale Railway pod. Plan Phase 3 hunts each
 systematically.

 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 Session 54 — Fix Sprint: BUG 3 (Queue Column Corruption) Investigation + Fix Plan

 Context

 Session 53's Fire Drill Audit uncovered 5 silent-failure bugs. BUG 3 is the highest-priority blocker: the Prospecting Queue contains rows written in non-canonical column layouts, so /prospect_all silently

 can't see them. 8 rows from tonight's F2 run + 1912 pre-existing rows (some dating to 2026-03-20) are affected. Running Stages 6-8 carryover without a fix would compound the damage. This plan investigates,

 repairs existing damage, and fixes the writer(s) responsible.

 Canonical layout (20 cols, from tools/district_prospector.py:PROSPECT_COLUMNS + add_district at 2482-2564):

 1 State | 2 Account Name | 3 Email | 4 First Name | 5 Last Name | 6 Deal Level | 7 Parent District |

 8 Name Key | 9 Strategy | 10 Source | 11 Status | 12 Priority | 13 Date Added | 14 Date Approved |

 15 Sequence Doc URL | 16 Est. Enrollment | 17 School Count | 18 Total Licenses | 19 Signal ID | 20 Notes

 What I found in this planning pass that wasn't in the memory

 Session 53 audited add_district and external callers but never audited the five internal _write_rows callers inside district_prospector.py . Four of them have the bug class that explains most of the historical

 damage. This cuts the investigation scope dramatically:

 ┌──────────────────────────────────┬───────────┬────────────┬─────────────────────────┐

 │ Function │ Line │ Row length │ Missing Signal ID slot? │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ discover_districts │ 1141–1161 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_upward_targets │ 1229–1249 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_intra_district_expansion │ 1461–1482 │ 20 │ ✅ ok │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_closed_lost_targets │ 1680–1700 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_cold_license_requests │ 2327–2347 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 └──────────────────────────────────┴───────────┴────────────┴─────────────────────────┘

 Plus proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415–435) also writes 19. That's five writers producing "canonical-minus-Signal-ID" rows for strategies cold , upward , winback ,

 cold_license_request , proximity . The Signal ID column was added in commit 33e34f6 (early April). These callers were never updated. This explains the Kennedy Junior High winback row from 2026-03-20 that the

 memory describes.

 Also found: _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 reads A:S (19 cols) and writes back via padded[:len(headers)] — it silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update. Separate latent bug in the

 same file.

 Also found: migrate_prospect_columns at district_prospector.py:173-251 has a _KNOWN_STRATEGIES set that only lists 8 strategies ( upward, cold, winback, cold_license_request, trigger, sequence_reengagement,

 webinar_attendee, webinar_missed ). Missing ALL Session 49+ strategies: competitor_displacement, csta_partnership, charter_cmo, cte_center, private_school_network, compliance_gap, intra_district,

 cs_funding_recipient, homeschool_coop, proximity, esa_cluster . The migrator therefore silently passes new-strategy scrambled rows through as-is. A secondary gap.

 The part that's still a mystery

 The memory describes tonight's 8 F2 rows as having an even more scrambled fingerprint than the 19-element writers would produce:

 col 1: TX ← state

 col 2: Lackland ISD ← name

 col 3: competitor_displacement ← strategy (should be col 9)

 col 4: 654 ← priority (should be col 12)

 col 5: signal ← source (should be col 10)

 col 6: 1035 ← enrollment (should be col 16)

 col 7: Competitor: ... ← notes (should be col 20)

 col 8-12: empty

 col 13: lackland ← name_key (should be col 8)

 col 14: pending ← status (should be col 11)

 col 15: 2026-04-10 00:51 ← date_added (should be col 13)

 col 16-19: empty

 col 20: competitor_lackland... ← signal_id (should be col 19)

 That's 11 filled cells in a specific pattern — not a 19-element append (which would fill cols 1–19 contiguously with Notes landing at col 19). No writer in the current repo produces this. Working hypotheses in

 order of likelihood:

 1. The memory misreads the row. The /prospect_all visibility failure could have a simpler explanation (e.g., Status is actually correct at col 11 but /prospect_all filters on Date Approved being empty, or a

 similar read-side bug). The fastest test is re-reading row 1947 directly.

 2. Stale Railway worker running a pre-Session-34 layout (State, Name, Strategy, Priority, Source, Enrollment, Notes…). Confirmable via Railway deployment logs for the 00:48–00:52 UTC window on 2026-04-10.

 3. A deleted code path still reachable. Deleted versions of _write_rows callers may have written rows in the old 11-value pattern. git log --all --diff-filter=D on the relevant files will surface them.

 Execution plan — tight phases, stop-and-verify between each

 Phase 0 — Preflight (< 15 min, read-only)

 1. Flip kill switches to False for the duration of investigation: ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN , ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY . ( ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN , ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN already False per commit 3431323 .) No new

 writes to the queue from any scheduled scanner until Phase 6 lands.

 2. Confirm Railway state: current singleton deployment is on commit 7c345a07 (or newer). Pull Railway deployment log for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC and check for rolling-deploy overlap. Record result in plan

 annotations.

 3. Confirm Tulsa PS draft status (scheduled Tuesday 8:05 AM) — no action beyond noting whether it landed.

 4. Do not touch Pittsburgh PS (PA) or Archdiocese of Chicago (IL) queue rows — held for Steven's manual review.

 Phase 1 — Verify the memory's description of the Lackland row (< 10 min, read-only)

 Decision gate:

 - If memory is accurate (Strategy at col 3, Status at col 14, 11-value pattern): continue to Phase 2's "old layout hunt".

 - If memory is inaccurate (e.g., these rows are actually 19-element writes from a 19-element writer we haven't yet traced to F2): skip the ghost-writer hunt, jump straight to Phase 3 repair + Phase 5 writer

 fix for the 19-element bugs.

 Phase 2 — Scope the historical damage (< 30 min, read-only)

 Single Python script (no "harness," just a script) that reads every row from TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE via service account and classifies each by fingerprint. Output table: count by fingerprint × strategy × source ×

 year-month.

 Fingerprints to detect:

 - F_canonical: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Signal ID at idx 18, Notes at idx 19

 - F_19col: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Notes at idx 18, idx 19 empty (the 19-element bug)

 - F_lackland: Strategy at idx 2, Priority at idx 3, Source at idx 4, Est Enrollment at idx 5, Notes at idx 6, Name Key at idx 12, Status at idx 13, Date at idx 14, Signal ID at idx 19

 - F_legacy16: Strategy at idx 5 (the target of the existing migration)

 - F_unknown: anything else

 Deliverable: row counts per fingerprint, oldest+newest date per fingerprint, strategies observed per fingerprint, 3 sample rows per fingerprint. If any F_unknown group has meaningful size, inspect a sample to

 classify it before moving on.

 This classifier is the same code the Phase 3 repair script uses — write it once, reuse.

 Phase 3 — Snapshot + repair script (dry-run first)

 1. Snapshot first. Before any write, copy TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE to a new tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 via a spreadsheets.sheets.copyTo API call (or a clear+update into a new tab). This is

 non-negotiable — without a backup, any repair bug is unrecoverable.

 2. Write scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py :

 - Uses the Phase 2 classifier to assign a fingerprint to every row

 - For each fingerprint, defines a deterministic index→index remap to canonical positions

 - Writes a dry-run report to stdout: "N rows of F_19col would be rewritten; 3 sample before/after" (per fingerprint)

 - Only after operator approval, does a clear+update of the tab using the migrate_prospect_columns pattern (lines 228–239) as reference — same atomic-ish pattern Scout already uses

 3. Remap rules:

 - F_19col → F_canonical: row[:18] + [""] + [row[18]]. (Same logic the existing migrator uses for the 19-col → 20-col case.)

 - F_lackland → F_canonical: rebuild from known positions: new[0]=old[0] , new[1]=old[1] , new[5]="" , new[6]="" , new[7]=old[12] , new[8]=old[2] , new[9]=old[4] , new[10]=old[13] , new[11]=old[3] , new[12]=old[14] ,

 new[15]=old[5] , new[18]=old[19] , new[19]=old[6] , all other positions = "" . Document these explicitly in the script as constants, not magic numbers scattered through code.

 - F_legacy16 → F_canonical: already handled by existing migrate_prospect_columns ; reuse its logic, don't reinvent.

 4. Dry-run-then-commit flow: The script must have two modes — --dry-run (default) prints diff only, --apply actually writes. No --apply without reviewing --dry-run output first.

 5. Failure mode: If the apply step crashes mid-write, the backup tab is the recovery path. Document the recovery command in the script's docstring.

 Phase 4 — Fix the writers

 Patch all five 19-element writers to include the Signal ID slot before Notes. One commit per file for surgical rollback:

 - tools/district_prospector.py : fix discover_districts:1141-1161 , suggest_upward_targets:1229-1249 , suggest_closed_lost_targets:1680-1700 , suggest_cold_license_requests:2327-2347 . Add "", #

 Signal ID at the correct position (one line above Notes) in each.

 - tools/proximity_engine.py : fix add_proximity_prospects:415-435 the same way.

 - Also fix _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 : change the read range from A:S to A:T and ensure the write preserves col 20. (Same commit as district_prospector fix.)

 - Extend migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES ( district_prospector.py:181 ) to include every Session 49+ strategy listed above. This is belt-and-suspenders protection against future legacy-row drift.

 If Phase 1 found the Lackland layout is real , additionally:

 - git log -p --all --diff-filter=D -- tools/district_prospector.py tools/signal_processor.py looking for deleted row-construction code that produces the 11-value pattern. If found, confirm it has no current

 callers (it shouldn't). If it does have callers, fix them.

 - Add stack-trace logging to _write_rows as a temporary diagnostic commit; deploy; force-run F2 with kill switch ON; capture evidence; revert the logging commit. Only do this if the Lackland fingerprint

 survives Phase 3 repair (i.e., new F_lackland rows appear post-repair).

 Phase 5 — Apply the repair

 1. Run repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py --dry-run against live sheet. Review stdout diff.

 2. Spot-check 3 specific rows manually in the diff: Lackland ISD, Kennedy Junior High (winback, 2026-03-20), one proximity row.

 3. If diff looks correct, run --apply . Sheet clears + rewrites.

 4. Immediately verify:

 - /prospect_all in Telegram returns Lackland ISD, Mansfield TX, Naperville 203 IL, and the other 5 F2 rows

 - /prospect_all count delta ≈ 1920 (the 8 F2 + ~1912 pre-existing that are now readable)

 - Backup tab is untouched and still readable

 Phase 6 — Re-enable scanners + sentinel test

 1. Deploy the Phase 4 commits (one per file).

 2. Flip ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN back to True . Leave F4/F5 off until their own plans in the sprint.

 3. Run F2 manually for one state (IL or TX). Wait for completion.

 4. Phase 2 classifier re-run: confirm any new rows fingerprint as F_canonical . If yes, BUG 3 is fixed. If no, the ghost writer is still out there and Phase 4's stack-trace diagnostic kicks in.

 Exit criteria (BUG 3 is DONE when all of these hold)

 1. Phase 2 classifier shows zero rows in any fingerprint other than F_canonical after repair

 2. /prospect_all returns the 8 F2 rows from tonight (Lackland, Mansfield, Naperville 203, etc.) with Status="pending" and correct Priority

 3. Manual F2 run in Phase 6 produces rows that fingerprint as F_canonical

 4. Backup tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 exists and is readable

 5. Pittsburgh PS and Archdiocese rows are still intact and untouched (double-check via direct read)

 6. scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py is committed and its docstring documents the recovery procedure

 7. All five writer fixes are committed, each in its own commit

 Critical files (reference only, not exhaustive change list)

 - tools/district_prospector.py — writers at 1141, 1229, 1461, 1680, 2327; infrastructure at 144 ( _ensure_tab ), 173 ( migrate_prospect_columns ), 426 ( _write_rows ), 440 ( _update_status ), 2482 ( add_district )

 - tools/proximity_engine.py:394-441 — add_proximity_prospects writer

 - tools/signal_processor.py:3748-3764 — F2 auto-queue block (calls add_district , verified canonical)

 - scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py — new file (Phase 3)

 - agent/main.py:2996 — /prospect_add dispatch (verified canonical)

 Out of scope for this plan

 BUGS 1, 2, 4, 5 (separate plans during the same sprint). Stages 6-8 Session 52 carryover (blocked on BUG 3 completion). Any new feature, scanner, or strategy.

 Questions before execution

 1. Snapshot method: copy to a new tab ( BACKUP_YYYYMMDD ) or download to a local JSON? Recommend the new tab — faster to restore from, no local file state.

 2. Railway evidence: do we have the Railway CLI or API access needed to pull deployment logs for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC in Phase 0? If not, Phase 0 step 2 drops to "best effort" and we rely on Phase 1

 verification.

 3. Stack-trace diagnostic deploy: acceptable to deploy a temporary debug-logging commit to Railway only if Phase 1 confirms Lackland layout is real AND Phase 4 archaeology finds no deleted writer? (Would

 revert the same day.)

 ---

 Changes from the prior version + why

 1. Discovered the real bug class before writing phases. Prior version delegated the "audit internal callers" to Phase 3. That was the entire investigation. Moving it into the planning pass itself cut five

 unknowns down to one (the Lackland mystery) and lets the plan lead with evidence instead of speculation.

 2. Dropped the Serper-replay harness. It belongs in the BUG 1 (F4 query redesign) plan, not here. BUG 3 doesn't need to replay Serper — it needs to read the queue, call add_district in isolation, and inspect

 results. That's a 20-line script, not a harness.

 3. Reordered phases to lead with a ground-truth check. Phase 1 in the new plan (re-read row 1947 to verify memory) is a 10-minute read-only sanity check that could collapse half the plan. Prior version had

 this buried inside Phase 4 "capture evidence".

 4. Made the snapshot a first-class phase step. Prior plan mentioned backups in passing. This one makes backing up to a new tab a hard prerequisite before any repair write, with a documented recovery command.

 That's how you avoid a "helpful" repair script destroying 1912 rows.

 5. Explicit dry-run-then-apply split. Repair script is --dry-run by default; --apply is gated on operator review of the dry-run diff. Prior plan said "dry run to stdout" but didn't make it a hard flag-gate.

 6. Kill switch state is explicit. Phase 0 flips F2 and F8 off before any investigation and Phase 6 flips F2 back on after verification. Prior plan said "consider flipping" — ambiguous.

 7. Exit criteria are quantified and measurable. Prior plan had "verification" bullet points that mixed "it works" with "we believe it works". New version is 7 specific, checkable conditions.

 8. Repair fingerprint math is spelled out as index remaps, not hand-waved. The prior plan gestured at "position substitution"; this one gives the explicit index→index mapping for each fingerprint. That's the

 part most likely to have a bug, so it needs to be reviewable before executing.

 9. Audit of _update_status and migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES folded in. Both were listed as "latent bugs found along the way" in the prior version. New version groups them into Phase 4 as part of

 the writer fix, because they touch the same file and shipping them separately is pointless churn.

 10. Added Lackland-mystery branch. Instead of treating the ghost writer as THE central problem, the plan now acknowledges it may be the memory mis-describing row 1947. Phase 1 collapses the whole branch in one

 read. If the memory was accurate, Phase 4's archaeology + stack-trace diagnostic kicks in as a contingency. If the memory was wrong, we avoid a multi-day rabbit hole.

 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌

 ⏺ Key findings before exiting plan mode:

 - Agent 1's proximity bug is real but separate. proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415-435) writes 19 cols, missing Signal ID. That's a latent off-by-one, but its layout

 (Strategy at col 9) does NOT match the Lackland fingerprint (Strategy at col 3). Not the BUG 3 root cause — but fixing it is in Phase 6.

 - Agent 2's "F8 missing kwargs" theory is backwards. F8 Archdiocese was confirmed canonical by direct sheet read in Session 53; F2 Lackland was the scrambled one. Agent 2 misread the attribution. Ruled out.

 - _update_status (district_prospector.py:440-471) reads A:S (19 cols) and silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update — another latent bug. In Phase 6.

 - migrate_prospect_columns (district_prospector.py:173) only knows 4 legacy strategies in _KNOWN_STRATEGIES — missing ALL 10 Session 49+ strategies. Ghost writer's rows would be kept as-is even if the

 migration runs.

 - The Lackland fingerprint matches NO current code path. Either a deleted code path is still somehow routed to, a CSV-import path is miswriting, or there's a stale Railway pod. Plan Phase 3 hunts each

 systematically.

 1. The memory misreads the row. The /prospect_all visibility failure could have a simpler explanation (e.g., Status is actually correct at col 11 but /prospect_all filters on Date Approved being empty, or a

 similar read-side bug). The fastest test is re-reading row 1947 directly.

 2. Stale Railway worker running a pre-Session-34 layout (State, Name, Strategy, Priority, Source, Enrollment, Notes…). Confirmable via Railway deployment logs for the 00:48–00:52 UTC window on 2026-04-10.

 3. A deleted code path still reachable. Deleted versions of _write_rows callers may have written rows in the old 11-value pattern. git log --all --diff-filter=D on the relevant files will surface them.

 Execution plan — tight phases, stop-and-verify between each

 Phase 0 — Preflight (< 15 min, read-only)

 1. Flip kill switches to False for the duration of investigation: ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN , ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY . ( ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN , ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN already False per commit 3431323 .) No new

 writes to the queue from any scheduled scanner until Phase 6 lands.

 2. Confirm Railway state: current singleton deployment is on commit 7c345a07 (or newer). Pull Railway deployment log for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC and check for rolling-deploy overlap. Record result in

 plan annotations.

 3. Confirm Tulsa PS draft status (scheduled Tuesday 8:05 AM) — no action beyond noting whether it landed.

 4. Do not touch Pittsburgh PS (PA) or Archdiocese of Chicago (IL) queue rows — held for Steven's manual review.

 Phase 1 — Verify the memory's description of the Lackland row (< 10 min, read-only)

 Decision gate:

 - If memory is accurate (Strategy at col 3, Status at col 14, 11-value pattern): continue to Phase 2's "old layout hunt".

 - If memory is inaccurate (e.g., these rows are actually 19-element writes from a 19-element writer we haven't yet traced to F2): skip the ghost-writer hunt, jump straight to Phase 3 repair + Phase 5 writer

 fix for the 19-element bugs.

 Phase 2 — Scope the historical damage (< 30 min, read-only)

 Single Python script (no "harness," just a script) that reads every row from TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE via service account and classifies each by fingerprint. Output table: count by fingerprint × strategy × source

 × year-month.

 Fingerprints to detect:

 - F_canonical: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Signal ID at idx 18, Notes at idx 19

 - F_19col: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Notes at idx 18, idx 19 empty (the 19-element bug)

 - F_lackland: Strategy at idx 2, Priority at idx 3, Source at idx 4, Est Enrollment at idx 5, Notes at idx 6, Name Key at idx 12, Status at idx 13, Date at idx 14, Signal ID at idx 19

 - F_legacy16: Strategy at idx 5 (the target of the existing migration)

 - F_unknown: anything else

 Deliverable: row counts per fingerprint, oldest+newest date per fingerprint, strategies observed per fingerprint, 3 sample rows per fingerprint. If any F_unknown group has meaningful size, inspect a sample

 to classify it before moving on.

 This classifier is the same code the Phase 3 repair script uses — write it once, reuse.

 Phase 3 — Snapshot + repair script (dry-run first)

 1. Snapshot first. Before any write, copy TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE to a new tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 via a spreadsheets.sheets.copyTo API call (or a clear+update into a new tab). This is

 non-negotiable — without a backup, any repair bug is unrecoverable.

 2. Write scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py :

 - Uses the Phase 2 classifier to assign a fingerprint to every row

 - For each fingerprint, defines a deterministic index→index remap to canonical positions

 - Writes a dry-run report to stdout: "N rows of F_19col would be rewritten; 3 sample before/after" (per fingerprint)

 - Only after operator approval, does a clear+update of the tab using the migrate_prospect_columns pattern (lines 228–239) as reference — same atomic-ish pattern Scout already uses

 3. Remap rules:

 - F_19col → F_canonical: row[:18] + [""] + [row[18]]. (Same logic the existing migrator uses for the 19-col → 20-col case.)

 - F_lackland → F_canonical: rebuild from known positions: new[0]=old[0] , new[1]=old[1] , new[5]="" , new[6]="" , new[7]=old[12] , new[8]=old[2] , new[9]=old[4] , new[10]=old[13] , new[11]=old[3] ,

 new[12]=old[14] , new[15]=old[5] , new[18]=old[19] , new[19]=old[6] , all other positions = "" . Document these explicitly in the script as constants, not magic numbers scattered through code.

 - F_legacy16 → F_canonical: already handled by existing migrate_prospect_columns ; reuse its logic, don't reinvent.

 4. Dry-run-then-commit flow: The script must have two modes — --dry-run (default) prints diff only, --apply actually writes. No --apply without reviewing --dry-run output first.

 5. Failure mode: If the apply step crashes mid-write, the backup tab is the recovery path. Document the recovery command in the script's docstring.

 Phase 4 — Fix the writers

 Patch all five 19-element writers to include the Signal ID slot before Notes. One commit per file for surgical rollback:

 - tools/district_prospector.py : fix discover_districts:1141-1161 , suggest_upward_targets:1229-1249 , suggest_closed_lost_targets:1680-1700 , suggest_cold_license_requests:2327-2347 . Add "", #

 Signal ID at the correct position (one line above Notes) in each.

 - tools/proximity_engine.py : fix add_proximity_prospects:415-435 the same way.

 - Also fix _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 : change the read range from A:S to A:T and ensure the write preserves col 20. (Same commit as district_prospector fix.)

 - Extend migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES ( district_prospector.py:181 ) to include every Session 49+ strategy listed above. This is belt-and-suspenders protection against future legacy-row drift.

 If Phase 1 found the Lackland layout is real , additionally:

 - git log -p --all --diff-filter=D -- tools/district_prospector.py tools/signal_processor.py looking for deleted row-construction code that produces the 11-value pattern. If found, confirm it has no current

 callers (it shouldn't). If it does have callers, fix them.

 - Add stack-trace logging to _write_rows as a temporary diagnostic commit; deploy; force-run F2 with kill switch ON; capture evidence; revert the logging commit. Only do this if the Lackland fingerprint

 survives Phase 3 repair (i.e., new F_lackland rows appear post-repair).

 Phase 5 — Apply the repair

 1. Run repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py --dry-run against live sheet. Review stdout diff.

 2. Spot-check 3 specific rows manually in the diff: Lackland ISD, Kennedy Junior High (winback, 2026-03-20), one proximity row.

 3. If diff looks correct, run --apply . Sheet clears + rewrites.

 4. Immediately verify:

 - /prospect_all in Telegram returns Lackland ISD, Mansfield TX, Naperville 203 IL, and the other 5 F2 rows

 - /prospect_all count delta ≈ 1920 (the 8 F2 + ~1912 pre-existing that are now readable)

 - Backup tab is untouched and still readable

 Phase 6 — Re-enable scanners + sentinel test

 1. Deploy the Phase 4 commits (one per file).

 2. Flip ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN back to True . Leave F4/F5 off until their own plans in the sprint.

 3. Run F2 manually for one state (IL or TX). Wait for completion.

 4. Phase 2 classifier re-run: confirm any new rows fingerprint as F_canonical . If yes, BUG 3 is fixed. If no, the ghost writer is still out there and Phase 4's stack-trace diagnostic kicks in.

 Exit criteria (BUG 3 is DONE when all of these hold)

 1. Phase 2 classifier shows zero rows in any fingerprint other than F_canonical after repair

 2. /prospect_all returns the 8 F2 rows from tonight (Lackland, Mansfield, Naperville 203, etc.) with Status="pending" and correct Priority

 3. Manual F2 run in Phase 6 produces rows that fingerprint as F_canonical

 4. Backup tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 exists and is readable

 5. Pittsburgh PS and Archdiocese rows are still intact and untouched (double-check via direct read)

 6. scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py is committed and its docstring documents the recovery procedure

 7. All five writer fixes are committed, each in its own commit

 Critical files (reference only, not exhaustive change list)

 - tools/district_prospector.py — writers at 1141, 1229, 1461, 1680, 2327; infrastructure at 144 ( _ensure_tab ), 173 ( migrate_prospect_columns ), 426 ( _write_rows ), 440 ( _update_status ), 2482 ( add_district )

 - tools/proximity_engine.py:394-441 — add_proximity_prospects writer

 - tools/signal_processor.py:3748-3764 — F2 auto-queue block (calls add_district , verified canonical)

 - scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py — new file (Phase 3)

 - agent/main.py:2996 — /prospect_add dispatch (verified canonical)

 Out of scope for this plan

 BUGS 1, 2, 4, 5 (separate plans during the same sprint). Stages 6-8 Session 52 carryover (blocked on BUG 3 completion). Any new feature, scanner, or strategy.

 Questions before execution

 1. Snapshot method: copy to a new tab ( BACKUP_YYYYMMDD ) or download to a local JSON? Recommend the new tab — faster to restore from, no local file state.

 2. Railway evidence: do we have the Railway CLI or API access needed to pull deployment logs for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC in Phase 0? If not, Phase 0 step 2 drops to "best effort" and we rely on Phase 1

 verification.

 3. Stack-trace diagnostic deploy: acceptable to deploy a temporary debug-logging commit to Railway only if Phase 1 confirms Lackland layout is real AND Phase 4 archaeology finds no deleted writer? (Would

 revert the same day.)

 ---

 Changes from the prior version + why

 1. Discovered the real bug class before writing phases. Prior version delegated the "audit internal callers" to Phase 3. That was the entire investigation. Moving it into the planning pass itself cut five

 unknowns down to one (the Lackland mystery) and lets the plan lead with evidence instead of speculation.

 2. Dropped the Serper-replay harness. It belongs in the BUG 1 (F4 query redesign) plan, not here. BUG 3 doesn't need to replay Serper — it needs to read the queue, call add_district in isolation, and

 inspect results. That's a 20-line script, not a harness.

 3. Reordered phases to lead with a ground-truth check. Phase 1 in the new plan (re-read row 1947 to verify memory) is a 10-minute read-only sanity check that could collapse half the plan. Prior version had

 this buried inside Phase 4 "capture evidence".

 4. Made the snapshot a first-class phase step. Prior plan mentioned backups in passing. This one makes backing up to a new tab a hard prerequisite before any repair write, with a documented recovery

 command. That's how you avoid a "helpful" repair script destroying 1912 rows.

 5. Explicit dry-run-then-apply split. Repair script is --dry-run by default; --apply is gated on operator review of the dry-run diff. Prior plan said "dry run to stdout" but didn't make it a hard flag-gate.

 6. Kill switch state is explicit. Phase 0 flips F2 and F8 off before any investigation and Phase 6 flips F2 back on after verification. Prior plan said "consider flipping" — ambiguous.

 7. Exit criteria are quantified and measurable. Prior plan had "verification" bullet points that mixed "it works" with "we believe it works". New version is 7 specific, checkable conditions.

 8. Repair fingerprint math is spelled out as index remaps, not hand-waved. The prior plan gestured at "position substitution"; this one gives the explicit index→index mapping for each fingerprint. That's

 the part most likely to have a bug, so it needs to be reviewable before executing.

 9. Audit of _update_status and migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES folded in. Both were listed as "latent bugs found along the way" in the prior version. New version groups them into Phase 4 as part

 of the writer fix, because they touch the same file and shipping them separately is pointless churn.

 10. Added Lackland-mystery branch. Instead of treating the ghost writer as THE central problem, the plan now acknowledges it may be the memory mis-describing row 1947. Phase 1 collapses the whole branch in

 one read. If the memory was accurate, Phase 4's archaeology + stack-trace diagnostic kicks in as a contingency. If the memory was wrong, we avoid a multi-day rabbit hole.

 ⏺ Key findings before exiting plan mode:

 - Agent 1's proximity bug is real but separate. proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415-435) writes 19 cols, missing Signal ID. That's a latent off-by-one, but its layout

 (Strategy at col 9) does NOT match the Lackland fingerprint (Strategy at col 3). Not the BUG 3 root cause — but fixing it is in Phase 6.

 - Agent 2's "F8 missing kwargs" theory is backwards. F8 Archdiocese was confirmed canonical by direct sheet read in Session 53; F2 Lackland was the scrambled one. Agent 2 misread the attribution. Ruled out.

 - _update_status (district_prospector.py:440-471) reads A:S (19 cols) and silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update — another latent bug. In Phase 6.

 - migrate_prospect_columns (district_prospector.py:173) only knows 4 legacy strategies in _KNOWN_STRATEGIES — missing ALL 10 Session 49+ strategies. Ghost writer's rows would be kept as-is even if the

 migration runs.

 - The Lackland fingerprint matches NO current code path. Either a deleted code path is still somehow routed to, a CSV-import path is miswriting, or there's a stale Railway pod. Plan Phase 3 hunts each

 systematically.

 Session 54 — Fix Sprint: BUG 3 (Queue Column Corruption) Investigation + Fix Plan

 Context

 Session 53's Fire Drill Audit uncovered 5 silent-failure bugs. BUG 3 is the highest-priority blocker: the Prospecting Queue contains rows written in non-canonical column layouts, so /prospect_all silently

 can't see them. 8 rows from tonight's F2 run + 1912 pre-existing rows (some dating to 2026-03-20) are affected. Running Stages 6-8 carryover without a fix would compound the damage. This plan investigates,

 repairs existing damage, and fixes the writer(s) responsible.

 Canonical layout (20 cols, from tools/district_prospector.py:PROSPECT_COLUMNS + add_district at 2482-2564):

 1 State | 2 Account Name | 3 Email | 4 First Name | 5 Last Name | 6 Deal Level | 7 Parent District |

 8 Name Key | 9 Strategy | 10 Source | 11 Status | 12 Priority | 13 Date Added | 14 Date Approved |

 15 Sequence Doc URL | 16 Est. Enrollment | 17 School Count | 18 Total Licenses | 19 Signal ID | 20 Notes

 What I found in this planning pass that wasn't in the memory

 Session 53 audited add_district and external callers but never audited the five internal _write_rows callers inside district_prospector.py . Four of them have the bug class that explains most of the

 historical damage. This cuts the investigation scope dramatically:

 ┌──────────────────────────────────┬───────────┬────────────┬─────────────────────────┐

 │ Function │ Line │ Row length │ Missing Signal ID slot? │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ discover_districts │ 1141–1161 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_upward_targets │ 1229–1249 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_intra_district_expansion │ 1461–1482 │ 20 │ ✅ ok │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_closed_lost_targets │ 1680–1700 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_cold_license_requests │ 2327–2347 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 └──────────────────────────────────┴───────────┴────────────┴─────────────────────────┘

 Plus proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415–435) also writes 19. That's five writers producing "canonical-minus-Signal-ID" rows for strategies cold , upward , winback ,

 cold_license_request , proximity . The Signal ID column was added in commit 33e34f6 (early April). These callers were never updated. This explains the Kennedy Junior High winback row from 2026-03-20 that the

 memory describes.

 Also found: _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 reads A:S (19 cols) and writes back via padded[:len(headers)] — it silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update. Separate latent bug in

 the same file.

 Also found: migrate_prospect_columns at district_prospector.py:173-251 has a _KNOWN_STRATEGIES set that only lists 8 strategies ( upward, cold, winback, cold_license_request, trigger, sequence_reengagement,

 webinar_attendee, webinar_missed ). Missing ALL Session 49+ strategies: competitor_displacement, csta_partnership, charter_cmo, cte_center, private_school_network, compliance_gap, intra_district,

 cs_funding_recipient, homeschool_coop, proximity, esa_cluster . The migrator therefore silently passes new-strategy scrambled rows through as-is. A secondary gap.

 The part that's still a mystery

 The memory describes tonight's 8 F2 rows as having an even more scrambled fingerprint than the 19-element writers would produce:

 col 1: TX ← state

 col 2: Lackland ISD ← name

 col 3: competitor_displacement ← strategy (should be col 9)

 col 4: 654 ← priority (should be col 12)

 col 5: signal ← source (should be col 10)

 col 6: 1035 ← enrollment (should be col 16)

 col 7: Competitor: ... ← notes (should be col 20)

 col 8-12: empty

 col 13: lackland ← name_key (should be col 8)

 col 14: pending ← status (should be col 11)

 col 15: 2026-04-10 00:51 ← date_added (should be col 13)

 col 16-19: empty

 col 20: competitor_lackland... ← signal_id (should be col 19)

 That's 11 filled cells in a specific pattern — not a 19-element append (which would fill cols 1–19 contiguously with Notes landing at col 19). No writer in the current repo produces this. Working hypotheses

 in order of likelihood:

 1. The memory misreads the row. The /prospect_all visibility failure could have a simpler explanation (e.g., Status is actually correct at col 11 but /prospect_all filters on Date Approved being empty, or a

 similar read-side bug). The fastest test is re-reading row 1947 directly.

 2. Stale Railway worker running a pre-Session-34 layout (State, Name, Strategy, Priority, Source, Enrollment, Notes…). Confirmable via Railway deployment logs for the 00:48–00:52 UTC window on 2026-04-10.

 3. A deleted code path still reachable. Deleted versions of _write_rows callers may have written rows in the old 11-value pattern. git log --all --diff-filter=D on the relevant files will surface them.

 Execution plan — tight phases, stop-and-verify between each

 Phase 0 — Preflight (< 15 min, read-only)

 1. Flip kill switches to False for the duration of investigation: ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN , ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY . ( ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN , ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN already False per commit 3431323 .) No new

 writes to the queue from any scheduled scanner until Phase 6 lands.

 2. Confirm Railway state: current singleton deployment is on commit 7c345a07 (or newer). Pull Railway deployment log for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC and check for rolling-deploy overlap. Record result in

 plan annotations.

 3. Confirm Tulsa PS draft status (scheduled Tuesday 8:05 AM) — no action beyond noting whether it landed.

 4. Do not touch Pittsburgh PS (PA) or Archdiocese of Chicago (IL) queue rows — held for Steven's manual review.

 Phase 1 — Verify the memory's description of the Lackland row (< 10 min, read-only)

 Decision gate:

 - If memory is accurate (Strategy at col 3, Status at col 14, 11-value pattern): continue to Phase 2's "old layout hunt".

 - If memory is inaccurate (e.g., these rows are actually 19-element writes from a 19-element writer we haven't yet traced to F2): skip the ghost-writer hunt, jump straight to Phase 3 repair + Phase 5 writer

 fix for the 19-element bugs.

 Phase 2 — Scope the historical damage (< 30 min, read-only)

 Single Python script (no "harness," just a script) that reads every row from TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE via service account and classifies each by fingerprint. Output table: count by fingerprint × strategy × source

 × year-month.

 Fingerprints to detect:

 - F_canonical: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Signal ID at idx 18, Notes at idx 19

 - F_19col: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Notes at idx 18, idx 19 empty (the 19-element bug)

 - F_lackland: Strategy at idx 2, Priority at idx 3, Source at idx 4, Est Enrollment at idx 5, Notes at idx 6, Name Key at idx 12, Status at idx 13, Date at idx 14, Signal ID at idx 19

 - F_legacy16: Strategy at idx 5 (the target of the existing migration)

 - F_unknown: anything else

 Deliverable: row counts per fingerprint, oldest+newest date per fingerprint, strategies observed per fingerprint, 3 sample rows per fingerprint. If any F_unknown group has meaningful size, inspect a sample

 to classify it before moving on.

 This classifier is the same code the Phase 3 repair script uses — write it once, reuse.

 Phase 3 — Snapshot + repair script (dry-run first)

 1. Snapshot first. Before any write, copy TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE to a new tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 via a spreadsheets.sheets.copyTo API call (or a clear+update into a new tab). This is

 non-negotiable — without a backup, any repair bug is unrecoverable.

 2. Write scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py :

 - Uses the Phase 2 classifier to assign a fingerprint to every row

 - For each fingerprint, defines a deterministic index→index remap to canonical positions

 - Writes a dry-run report to stdout: "N rows of F_19col would be rewritten; 3 sample before/after" (per fingerprint)

 - Only after operator approval, does a clear+update of the tab using the migrate_prospect_columns pattern (lines 228–239) as reference — same atomic-ish pattern Scout already uses

 3. Remap rules:

 - F_19col → F_canonical: row[:18] + [""] + [row[18]]. (Same logic the existing migrator uses for the 19-col → 20-col case.)

 - F_lackland → F_canonical: rebuild from known positions: new[0]=old[0] , new[1]=old[1] , new[5]="" , new[6]="" , new[7]=old[12] , new[8]=old[2] , new[9]=old[4] , new[10]=old[13] , new[11]=old[3] ,

 new[12]=old[14] , new[15]=old[5] , new[18]=old[19] , new[19]=old[6] , all other positions = "" . Document these explicitly in the script as constants, not magic numbers scattered through code.

 - F_legacy16 → F_canonical: already handled by existing migrate_prospect_columns ; reuse its logic, don't reinvent.

 4. Dry-run-then-commit flow: The script must have two modes — --dry-run (default) prints diff only, --apply actually writes. No --apply without reviewing --dry-run output first.

 5. Failure mode: If the apply step crashes mid-write, the backup tab is the recovery path. Document the recovery command in the script's docstring.

 Phase 4 — Fix the writers

 Patch all five 19-element writers to include the Signal ID slot before Notes. One commit per file for surgical rollback:

 - tools/district_prospector.py : fix discover_districts:1141-1161 , suggest_upward_targets:1229-1249 , suggest_closed_lost_targets:1680-1700 , suggest_cold_license_requests:2327-2347 . Add "", #

 Signal ID at the correct position (one line above Notes) in each.

 - tools/proximity_engine.py : fix add_proximity_prospects:415-435 the same way.

 - Also fix _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 : change the read range from A:S to A:T and ensure the write preserves col 20. (Same commit as district_prospector fix.)

 - Extend migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES ( district_prospector.py:181 ) to include every Session 49+ strategy listed above. This is belt-and-suspenders protection against future legacy-row drift.

 If Phase 1 found the Lackland layout is real , additionally:

 - git log -p --all --diff-filter=D -- tools/district_prospector.py tools/signal_processor.py looking for deleted row-construction code that produces the 11-value pattern. If found, confirm it has no current

 callers (it shouldn't). If it does have callers, fix them.

 - Add stack-trace logging to _write_rows as a temporary diagnostic commit; deploy; force-run F2 with kill switch ON; capture evidence; revert the logging commit. Only do this if the Lackland fingerprint

 survives Phase 3 repair (i.e., new F_lackland rows appear post-repair).

 Phase 5 — Apply the repair

 1. Run repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py --dry-run against live sheet. Review stdout diff.

 2. Spot-check 3 specific rows manually in the diff: Lackland ISD, Kennedy Junior High (winback, 2026-03-20), one proximity row.

 3. If diff looks correct, run --apply . Sheet clears + rewrites.

 4. Immediately verify:

 - /prospect_all in Telegram returns Lackland ISD, Mansfield TX, Naperville 203 IL, and the other 5 F2 rows

 - /prospect_all count delta ≈ 1920 (the 8 F2 + ~1912 pre-existing that are now readable)

 - Backup tab is untouched and still readable

 Phase 6 — Re-enable scanners + sentinel test

 1. Deploy the Phase 4 commits (one per file).

 2. Flip ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN back to True . Leave F4/F5 off until their own plans in the sprint.

 3. Run F2 manually for one state (IL or TX). Wait for completion.

 4. Phase 2 classifier re-run: confirm any new rows fingerprint as F_canonical . If yes, BUG 3 is fixed. If no, the ghost writer is still out there and Phase 4's stack-trace diagnostic kicks in.

 Exit criteria (BUG 3 is DONE when all of these hold)

 1. Phase 2 classifier shows zero rows in any fingerprint other than F_canonical after repair

 2. /prospect_all returns the 8 F2 rows from tonight (Lackland, Mansfield, Naperville 203, etc.) with Status="pending" and correct Priority

 3. Manual F2 run in Phase 6 produces rows that fingerprint as F_canonical

 4. Backup tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 exists and is readable

 5. Pittsburgh PS and Archdiocese rows are still intact and untouched (double-check via direct read)

 6. scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py is committed and its docstring documents the recovery procedure

 7. All five writer fixes are committed, each in its own commit

 Critical files (reference only, not exhaustive change list)

 - tools/district_prospector.py — writers at 1141, 1229, 1461, 1680, 2327; infrastructure at 144 ( _ensure_tab ), 173 ( migrate_prospect_columns ), 426 ( _write_rows ), 440 ( _update_status ), 2482 ( add_district )

 - tools/proximity_engine.py:394-441 — add_proximity_prospects writer

 - tools/signal_processor.py:3748-3764 — F2 auto-queue block (calls add_district , verified canonical)

 - scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py — new file (Phase 3)

 - agent/main.py:2996 — /prospect_add dispatch (verified canonical)

 Out of scope for this plan

 BUGS 1, 2, 4, 5 (separate plans during the same sprint). Stages 6-8 Session 52 carryover (blocked on BUG 3 completion). Any new feature, scanner, or strategy.

 Questions before execution

 1. Snapshot method: copy to a new tab ( BACKUP_YYYYMMDD ) or download to a local JSON? Recommend the new tab — faster to restore from, no local file state.

 2. Railway evidence: do we have the Railway CLI or API access needed to pull deployment logs for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC in Phase 0? If not, Phase 0 step 2 drops to "best effort" and we rely on Phase 1

 verification.

 3. Stack-trace diagnostic deploy: acceptable to deploy a temporary debug-logging commit to Railway only if Phase 1 confirms Lackland layout is real AND Phase 4 archaeology finds no deleted writer? (Would

 revert the same day.)

 ---

 Changes from the prior version + why

 1. Discovered the real bug class before writing phases. Prior version delegated the "audit internal callers" to Phase 3. That was the entire investigation. Moving it into the planning pass itself cut five

 unknowns down to one (the Lackland mystery) and lets the plan lead with evidence instead of speculation.

 2. Dropped the Serper-replay harness. It belongs in the BUG 1 (F4 query redesign) plan, not here. BUG 3 doesn't need to replay Serper — it needs to read the queue, call add_district in isolation, and

 inspect results. That's a 20-line script, not a harness.

 3. Reordered phases to lead with a ground-truth check. Phase 1 in the new plan (re-read row 1947 to verify memory) is a 10-minute read-only sanity check that could collapse half the plan. Prior version had

 this buried inside Phase 4 "capture evidence".

 4. Made the snapshot a first-class phase step. Prior plan mentioned backups in passing. This one makes backing up to a new tab a hard prerequisite before any repair write, with a documented recovery

 command. That's how you avoid a "helpful" repair script destroying 1912 rows.

 5. Explicit dry-run-then-apply split. Repair script is --dry-run by default; --apply is gated on operator review of the dry-run diff. Prior plan said "dry run to stdout" but didn't make it a hard flag-gate.

 6. Kill switch state is explicit. Phase 0 flips F2 and F8 off before any investigation and Phase 6 flips F2 back on after verification. Prior plan said "consider flipping" — ambiguous.

 7. Exit criteria are quantified and measurable. Prior plan had "verification" bullet points that mixed "it works" with "we believe it works". New version is 7 specific, checkable conditions.

 8. Repair fingerprint math is spelled out as index remaps, not hand-waved. The prior plan gestured at "position substitution"; this one gives the explicit index→index mapping for each fingerprint. That's

 the part most likely to have a bug, so it needs to be reviewable before executing.

 9. Audit of _update_status and migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES folded in. Both were listed as "latent bugs found along the way" in the prior version. New version groups them into Phase 4 as part

 of the writer fix, because they touch the same file and shipping them separately is pointless churn.

 10. Added Lackland-mystery branch. Instead of treating the ghost writer as THE central problem, the plan now acknowledges it may be the memory mis-describing row 1947. Phase 1 collapses the whole branch in

 one read. If the memory was accurate, Phase 4's archaeology + stack-trace diagnostic kicks in as a contingency. If the memory was wrong, we avoid a multi-day rabbit hole.

 seed queue which already ran).

 ⏺ Key findings before exiting plan mode:

 - Agent 1's proximity bug is real but separate. proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415-435) writes 19 cols, missing Signal ID. That's a latent off-by-one, but its layout

 (Strategy at col 9) does NOT match the Lackland fingerprint (Strategy at col 3). Not the BUG 3 root cause — but fixing it is in Phase 6.

 - Agent 2's "F8 missing kwargs" theory is backwards. F8 Archdiocese was confirmed canonical by direct sheet read in Session 53; F2 Lackland was the scrambled one. Agent 2 misread the attribution. Ruled out.

 - _update_status (district_prospector.py:440-471) reads A:S (19 cols) and silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update — another latent bug. In Phase 6.

 - migrate_prospect_columns (district_prospector.py:173) only knows 4 legacy strategies in _KNOWN_STRATEGIES — missing ALL 10 Session 49+ strategies. Ghost writer's rows would be kept as-is even if the

 migration runs.

 - The Lackland fingerprint matches NO current code path. Either a deleted code path is still somehow routed to, a CSV-import path is miswriting, or there's a stale Railway pod. Plan Phase 3 hunts each

 systematically.
