 Session 54 — Fix Sprint: BUG 3 (Queue Column Corruption) Investigation + Fix Plan

 Context

 Session 53's Fire Drill Audit uncovered 5 silent-failure bugs. BUG 3 is the highest-priority blocker: the Prospecting Queue contains rows written in non-canonical column layouts, so /prospect_all silently

 can't see them. 8 rows from tonight's F2 run + 1912 pre-existing rows (some dating to 2026-03-20) are affected. Running Stages 6-8 carryover without a fix would compound the damage. This plan investigates,

 repairs existing damage, and fixes the writer(s) responsible.

 Canonical layout (20 cols, from tools/district_prospector.py:PROSPECT_COLUMNS + add_district at 2482-2564):

 1 State | 2 Account Name | 3 Email | 4 First Name | 5 Last Name | 6 Deal Level | 7 Parent District |

 8 Name Key | 9 Strategy | 10 Source | 11 Status | 12 Priority | 13 Date Added | 14 Date Approved |

 15 Sequence Doc URL | 16 Est. Enrollment | 17 School Count | 18 Total Licenses | 19 Signal ID | 20 Notes

 What I found in this planning pass that wasn't in the memory

 Session 53 audited add_district and external callers but never audited the five internal _write_rows callers inside district_prospector.py . Four of them have the bug class that explains most of the

 historical damage. This cuts the investigation scope dramatically:

 ┌──────────────────────────────────┬───────────┬────────────┬─────────────────────────┐

 │ Function │ Line │ Row length │ Missing Signal ID slot? │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ discover_districts │ 1141–1161 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_upward_targets │ 1229–1249 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_intra_district_expansion │ 1461–1482 │ 20 │ ✅ ok │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_closed_lost_targets │ 1680–1700 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_cold_license_requests │ 2327–2347 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 └──────────────────────────────────┴───────────┴────────────┴─────────────────────────┘

 Plus proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415–435) also writes 19. That's five writers producing "canonical-minus-Signal-ID" rows for strategies cold , upward , winback ,

 cold_license_request , proximity . The Signal ID column was added in commit 33e34f6 (early April). These callers were never updated. This explains the Kennedy Junior High winback row from 2026-03-20 that the

 memory describes.

 Also found: _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 reads A:S (19 cols) and writes back via padded[:len(headers)] — it silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update. Separate latent bug in

 the same file.

 Also found: migrate_prospect_columns at district_prospector.py:173-251 has a _KNOWN_STRATEGIES set that only lists 8 strategies ( upward, cold, winback, cold_license_request, trigger, sequence_reengagement,

 webinar_attendee, webinar_missed ). Missing ALL Session 49+ strategies: competitor_displacement, csta_partnership, charter_cmo, cte_center, private_school_network, compliance_gap, intra_district,

 cs_funding_recipient, homeschool_coop, proximity, esa_cluster . The migrator therefore silently passes new-strategy scrambled rows through as-is. A secondary gap.

 The part that's still a mystery

 The memory describes tonight's 8 F2 rows as having an even more scrambled fingerprint than the 19-element writers would produce:

 col 1: TX ← state

 col 2: Lackland ISD ← name

 col 3: competitor_displacement ← strategy (should be col 9)

 col 4: 654 ← priority (should be col 12)

 col 5: signal ← source (should be col 10)

 col 6: 1035 ← enrollment (should be col 16)

 col 7: Competitor: ... ← notes (should be col 20)

 col 8-12: empty

 col 13: lackland ← name_key (should be col 8)

 col 14: pending ← status (should be col 11)

 col 15: 2026-04-10 00:51 ← date_added (should be col 13)

 col 16-19: empty

 col 20: competitor_lackland... ← signal_id (should be col 19)

 That's 11 filled cells in a specific pattern — not a 19-element append (which would fill cols 1–19 contiguously with Notes landing at col 19). No writer in the current repo produces this. Working hypotheses

 in order of likelihood:

 1. The memory misreads the row. The /prospect_all visibility failure could have a simpler explanation (e.g., Status is actually correct at col 11 but /prospect_all filters on Date Approved being empty, or a

 similar read-side bug). The fastest test is re-reading row 1947 directly.

 2. Stale Railway worker running a pre-Session-34 layout (State, Name, Strategy, Priority, Source, Enrollment, Notes…). Confirmable via Railway deployment logs for the 00:48–00:52 UTC window on 2026-04-10.

 3. A deleted code path still reachable. Deleted versions of _write_rows callers may have written rows in the old 11-value pattern. git log --all --diff-filter=D on the relevant files will surface them.

 Execution plan — tight phases, stop-and-verify between each

 Phase 0 — Preflight (< 15 min, read-only)

 1. Flip kill switches to False for the duration of investigation: ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN , ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY . ( ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN , ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN already False per commit 3431323 .) No new

 writes to the queue from any scheduled scanner until Phase 6 lands.

 2. Confirm Railway state: current singleton deployment is on commit 7c345a07 (or newer). Pull Railway deployment log for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC and check for rolling-deploy overlap. Record result in

 plan annotations.

 3. Confirm Tulsa PS draft status (scheduled Tuesday 8:05 AM) — no action beyond noting whether it landed.

 4. Do not touch Pittsburgh PS (PA) or Archdiocese of Chicago (IL) queue rows — held for Steven's manual review.

 Phase 1 — Verify the memory's description of the Lackland row (< 10 min, read-only)

 Decision gate:

 - If memory is accurate (Strategy at col 3, Status at col 14, 11-value pattern): continue to Phase 2's "old layout hunt".

 - If memory is inaccurate (e.g., these rows are actually 19-element writes from a 19-element writer we haven't yet traced to F2): skip the ghost-writer hunt, jump straight to Phase 3 repair + Phase 5 writer

 fix for the 19-element bugs.

 Phase 2 — Scope the historical damage (< 30 min, read-only)

 Single Python script (no "harness," just a script) that reads every row from TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE via service account and classifies each by fingerprint. Output table: count by fingerprint × strategy × source

 × year-month.

 Fingerprints to detect:

 - F_canonical: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Signal ID at idx 18, Notes at idx 19

 - F_19col: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Notes at idx 18, idx 19 empty (the 19-element bug)

 - F_lackland: Strategy at idx 2, Priority at idx 3, Source at idx 4, Est Enrollment at idx 5, Notes at idx 6, Name Key at idx 12, Status at idx 13, Date at idx 14, Signal ID at idx 19

 - F_legacy16: Strategy at idx 5 (the target of the existing migration)

 - F_unknown: anything else

 Deliverable: row counts per fingerprint, oldest+newest date per fingerprint, strategies observed per fingerprint, 3 sample rows per fingerprint. If any F_unknown group has meaningful size, inspect a sample

 to classify it before moving on.

 This classifier is the same code the Phase 3 repair script uses — write it once, reuse.

 Phase 3 — Snapshot + repair script (dry-run first)

 1. Snapshot first. Before any write, copy TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE to a new tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 via a spreadsheets.sheets.copyTo API call (or a clear+update into a new tab). This is

 non-negotiable — without a backup, any repair bug is unrecoverable.

 2. Write scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py :

 - Uses the Phase 2 classifier to assign a fingerprint to every row

 - For each fingerprint, defines a deterministic index→index remap to canonical positions

 - Writes a dry-run report to stdout: "N rows of F_19col would be rewritten; 3 sample before/after" (per fingerprint)

 - Only after operator approval, does a clear+update of the tab using the migrate_prospect_columns pattern (lines 228–239) as reference — same atomic-ish pattern Scout already uses

 3. Remap rules:

 - F_19col → F_canonical: row[:18] + [""] + [row[18]]. (Same logic the existing migrator uses for the 19-col → 20-col case.)

 - F_lackland → F_canonical: rebuild from known positions: new[0]=old[0] , new[1]=old[1] , new[5]="" , new[6]="" , new[7]=old[12] , new[8]=old[2] , new[9]=old[4] , new[10]=old[13] , new[11]=old[3] ,

 new[12]=old[14] , new[15]=old[5] , new[18]=old[19] , new[19]=old[6] , all other positions = "" . Document these explicitly in the script as constants, not magic numbers scattered through code.

 - F_legacy16 → F_canonical: already handled by existing migrate_prospect_columns ; reuse its logic, don't reinvent.

 4. Dry-run-then-commit flow: The script must have two modes — --dry-run (default) prints diff only, --apply actually writes. No --apply without reviewing --dry-run output first.

 5. Failure mode: If the apply step crashes mid-write, the backup tab is the recovery path. Document the recovery command in the script's docstring.

 Phase 4 — Fix the writers

 Patch all five 19-element writers to include the Signal ID slot before Notes. One commit per file for surgical rollback:

 - tools/district_prospector.py : fix discover_districts:1141-1161 , suggest_upward_targets:1229-1249 , suggest_closed_lost_targets:1680-1700 , suggest_cold_license_requests:2327-2347 . Add "", #

 Signal ID at the correct position (one line above Notes) in each.

 - tools/proximity_engine.py : fix add_proximity_prospects:415-435 the same way.

 - Also fix _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 : change the read range from A:S to A:T and ensure the write preserves col 20. (Same commit as district_prospector fix.)

 - Extend migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES ( district_prospector.py:181 ) to include every Session 49+ strategy listed above. This is belt-and-suspenders protection against future legacy-row drift.

 If Phase 1 found the Lackland layout is real , additionally:

 - git log -p --all --diff-filter=D -- tools/district_prospector.py tools/signal_processor.py looking for deleted row-construction code that produces the 11-value pattern. If found, confirm it has no current

 callers (it shouldn't). If it does have callers, fix them.

 - Add stack-trace logging to _write_rows as a temporary diagnostic commit; deploy; force-run F2 with kill switch ON; capture evidence; revert the logging commit. Only do this if the Lackland fingerprint

 survives Phase 3 repair (i.e., new F_lackland rows appear post-repair).

 Phase 5 — Apply the repair

 1. Run repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py --dry-run against live sheet. Review stdout diff.

 2. Spot-check 3 specific rows manually in the diff: Lackland ISD, Kennedy Junior High (winback, 2026-03-20), one proximity row.

 3. If diff looks correct, run --apply . Sheet clears + rewrites.

 4. Immediately verify:

 - /prospect_all in Telegram returns Lackland ISD, Mansfield TX, Naperville 203 IL, and the other 5 F2 rows

 - /prospect_all count delta ≈ 1920 (the 8 F2 + ~1912 pre-existing that are now readable)

 - Backup tab is untouched and still readable

 Phase 6 — Re-enable scanners + sentinel test

 1. Deploy the Phase 4 commits (one per file).

 2. Flip ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN back to True . Leave F4/F5 off until their own plans in the sprint.

 3. Run F2 manually for one state (IL or TX). Wait for completion.

 4. Phase 2 classifier re-run: confirm any new rows fingerprint as F_canonical . If yes, BUG 3 is fixed. If no, the ghost writer is still out there and Phase 4's stack-trace diagnostic kicks in.

 Exit criteria (BUG 3 is DONE when all of these hold)

 1. Phase 2 classifier shows zero rows in any fingerprint other than F_canonical after repair

 2. /prospect_all returns the 8 F2 rows from tonight (Lackland, Mansfield, Naperville 203, etc.) with Status="pending" and correct Priority

 3. Manual F2 run in Phase 6 produces rows that fingerprint as F_canonical

 4. Backup tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 exists and is readable

 5. Pittsburgh PS and Archdiocese rows are still intact and untouched (double-check via direct read)

 6. scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py is committed and its docstring documents the recovery procedure

 7. All five writer fixes are committed, each in its own commit

 Critical files (reference only, not exhaustive change list)

 - tools/district_prospector.py — writers at 1141, 1229, 1461, 1680, 2327; infrastructure at 144 ( _ensure_tab ), 173 ( migrate_prospect_columns ), 426 ( _write_rows ), 440 ( _update_status ), 2482 ( add_district )

 - tools/proximity_engine.py:394-441 — add_proximity_prospects writer

 - tools/signal_processor.py:3748-3764 — F2 auto-queue block (calls add_district , verified canonical)

 - scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py — new file (Phase 3)

 - agent/main.py:2996 — /prospect_add dispatch (verified canonical)

 Out of scope for this plan

 BUGS 1, 2, 4, 5 (separate plans during the same sprint). Stages 6-8 Session 52 carryover (blocked on BUG 3 completion). Any new feature, scanner, or strategy.

 Questions before execution

 1. Snapshot method: copy to a new tab ( BACKUP_YYYYMMDD ) or download to a local JSON? Recommend the new tab — faster to restore from, no local file state.

 2. Railway evidence: do we have the Railway CLI or API access needed to pull deployment logs for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC in Phase 0? If not, Phase 0 step 2 drops to "best effort" and we rely on Phase 1

 verification.

 3. Stack-trace diagnostic deploy: acceptable to deploy a temporary debug-logging commit to Railway only if Phase 1 confirms Lackland layout is real AND Phase 4 archaeology finds no deleted writer? (Would

 revert the same day.)

 ---

 Changes from the prior version + why

 1. Discovered the real bug class before writing phases. Prior version delegated the "audit internal callers" to Phase 3. That was the entire investigation. Moving it into the planning pass itself cut five

 unknowns down to one (the Lackland mystery) and lets the plan lead with evidence instead of speculation.

 2. Dropped the Serper-replay harness. It belongs in the BUG 1 (F4 query redesign) plan, not here. BUG 3 doesn't need to replay Serper — it needs to read the queue, call add_district in isolation, and

 inspect results. That's a 20-line script, not a harness.

 3. Reordered phases to lead with a ground-truth check. Phase 1 in the new plan (re-read row 1947 to verify memory) is a 10-minute read-only sanity check that could collapse half the plan. Prior version had

 this buried inside Phase 4 "capture evidence".

 4. Made the snapshot a first-class phase step. Prior plan mentioned backups in passing. This one makes backing up to a new tab a hard prerequisite before any repair write, with a documented recovery

 command. That's how you avoid a "helpful" repair script destroying 1912 rows.

 5. Explicit dry-run-then-apply split. Repair script is --dry-run by default; --apply is gated on operator review of the dry-run diff. Prior plan said "dry run to stdout" but didn't make it a hard flag-gate.

 6. Kill switch state is explicit. Phase 0 flips F2 and F8 off before any investigation and Phase 6 flips F2 back on after verification. Prior plan said "consider flipping" — ambiguous.

 7. Exit criteria are quantified and measurable. Prior plan had "verification" bullet points that mixed "it works" with "we believe it works". New version is 7 specific, checkable conditions.

 8. Repair fingerprint math is spelled out as index remaps, not hand-waved. The prior plan gestured at "position substitution"; this one gives the explicit index→index mapping for each fingerprint. That's

 the part most likely to have a bug, so it needs to be reviewable before executing.

 9. Audit of _update_status and migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES folded in. Both were listed as "latent bugs found along the way" in the prior version. New version groups them into Phase 4 as part

 of the writer fix, because they touch the same file and shipping them separately is pointless churn.

 10. Added Lackland-mystery branch. Instead of treating the ghost writer as THE central problem, the plan now acknowledges it may be the memory mis-describing row 1947. Phase 1 collapses the whole branch in

 one read. If the memory was accurate, Phase 4's archaeology + stack-trace diagnostic kicks in as a contingency. If the memory was wrong, we avoid a multi-day rabbit hole.

 ⏺ Plan approved. Starting execution with Phase 0 preflight.

 seed queue which already ran).

 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⏺ Key findings before exiting plan mode:

 - Agent 1's proximity bug is real but separate. proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415-435) writes 19 cols, missing Signal ID. That's a latent off-by-one, but its layout

 (Strategy at col 9) does NOT match the Lackland fingerprint (Strategy at col 3). Not the BUG 3 root cause — but fixing it is in Phase 6.

 - Agent 2's "F8 missing kwargs" theory is backwards. F8 Archdiocese was confirmed canonical by direct sheet read in Session 53; F2 Lackland was the scrambled one. Agent 2 misread the attribution. Ruled out.

 - _update_status (district_prospector.py:440-471) reads A:S (19 cols) and silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update — another latent bug. In Phase 6.

 - migrate_prospect_columns (district_prospector.py:173) only knows 4 legacy strategies in _KNOWN_STRATEGIES — missing ALL 10 Session 49+ strategies. Ghost writer's rows would be kept as-is even if the

 migration runs.

 - The Lackland fingerprint matches NO current code path. Either a deleted code path is still somehow routed to, a CSV-import path is miswriting, or there's a stale Railway pod. Plan Phase 3 hunts each

 systematically.

 ⎿  Tool use rejected

 Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⏺ Now I have the smoking gun — four internal _write_rows callers build 19-element rows missing Signal ID. Let me rewrite the plan.

 ⏺ Updated plan

 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⏺ User approved Claude's plan

 ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/sunny-riding-aurora.md · /plan to edit

 Session 54 — Fix Sprint: BUG 3 (Queue Column Corruption) Investigation + Fix Plan

 Context

 Session 53's Fire Drill Audit uncovered 5 silent-failure bugs. BUG 3 is the highest-priority blocker: the Prospecting Queue contains rows written in non-canonical column layouts, so /prospect_all silently

 can't see them. 8 rows from tonight's F2 run + 1912 pre-existing rows (some dating to 2026-03-20) are affected. Running Stages 6-8 carryover without a fix would compound the damage. This plan investigates,

 repairs existing damage, and fixes the writer(s) responsible.

 Canonical layout (20 cols, from tools/district_prospector.py:PROSPECT_COLUMNS + add_district at 2482-2564):

 1 State | 2 Account Name | 3 Email | 4 First Name | 5 Last Name | 6 Deal Level | 7 Parent District |

 8 Name Key | 9 Strategy | 10 Source | 11 Status | 12 Priority | 13 Date Added | 14 Date Approved |

 15 Sequence Doc URL | 16 Est. Enrollment | 17 School Count | 18 Total Licenses | 19 Signal ID | 20 Notes

 What I found in this planning pass that wasn't in the memory

 Session 53 audited add_district and external callers but never audited the five internal _write_rows callers inside district_prospector.py . Four of them have the bug class that explains most of the

 historical damage. This cuts the investigation scope dramatically:

 ┌──────────────────────────────────┬───────────┬────────────┬─────────────────────────┐

 │ Function │ Line │ Row length │ Missing Signal ID slot? │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ discover_districts │ 1141–1161 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_upward_targets │ 1229–1249 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_intra_district_expansion │ 1461–1482 │ 20 │ ✅ ok │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_closed_lost_targets │ 1680–1700 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_cold_license_requests │ 2327–2347 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 └──────────────────────────────────┴───────────┴────────────┴─────────────────────────┘

 Plus proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415–435) also writes 19. That's five writers producing "canonical-minus-Signal-ID" rows for strategies cold , upward , winback ,

 cold_license_request , proximity . The Signal ID column was added in commit 33e34f6 (early April). These callers were never updated. This explains the Kennedy Junior High winback row from 2026-03-20 that the

 memory describes.

 Also found: _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 reads A:S (19 cols) and writes back via padded[:len(headers)] — it silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update. Separate latent bug in

 the same file.

 Also found: migrate_prospect_columns at district_prospector.py:173-251 has a _KNOWN_STRATEGIES set that only lists 8 strategies ( upward, cold, winback, cold_license_request, trigger, sequence_reengagement,

 webinar_attendee, webinar_missed ). Missing ALL Session 49+ strategies: competitor_displacement, csta_partnership, charter_cmo, cte_center, private_school_network, compliance_gap, intra_district,

 cs_funding_recipient, homeschool_coop, proximity, esa_cluster . The migrator therefore silently passes new-strategy scrambled rows through as-is. A secondary gap.

 The part that's still a mystery

 The memory describes tonight's 8 F2 rows as having an even more scrambled fingerprint than the 19-element writers would produce:

 col 1: TX ← state

 col 2: Lackland ISD ← name

 col 3: competitor_displacement ← strategy (should be col 9)

 col 4: 654 ← priority (should be col 12)

 col 5: signal ← source (should be col 10)

 col 6: 1035 ← enrollment (should be col 16)

 col 7: Competitor: ... ← notes (should be col 20)

 col 8-12: empty

 col 13: lackland ← name_key (should be col 8)

 col 14: pending ← status (should be col 11)

 col 15: 2026-04-10 00:51 ← date_added (should be col 13)

 col 16-19: empty

 col 20: competitor_lackland... ← signal_id (should be col 19)

 That's 11 filled cells in a specific pattern — not a 19-element append (which would fill cols 1–19 contiguously with Notes landing at col 19). No writer in the current repo produces this. Working hypotheses

 in order of likelihood:

 1. The memory misreads the row. The /prospect_all visibility failure could have a simpler explanation (e.g., Status is actually correct at col 11 but /prospect_all filters on Date Approved being empty, or a

 similar read-side bug). The fastest test is re-reading row 1947 directly.

 2. Stale Railway worker running a pre-Session-34 layout (State, Name, Strategy, Priority, Source, Enrollment, Notes…). Confirmable via Railway deployment logs for the 00:48–00:52 UTC window on 2026-04-10.

 3. A deleted code path still reachable. Deleted versions of _write_rows callers may have written rows in the old 11-value pattern. git log --all --diff-filter=D on the relevant files will surface them.

 Execution plan — tight phases, stop-and-verify between each

 Phase 0 — Preflight (< 15 min, read-only)

 1. Flip kill switches to False for the duration of investigation: ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN , ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY . ( ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN , ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN already False per commit 3431323 .) No new

 writes to the queue from any scheduled scanner until Phase 6 lands.

 2. Confirm Railway state: current singleton deployment is on commit 7c345a07 (or newer). Pull Railway deployment log for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC and check for rolling-deploy overlap. Record result in

 plan annotations.

 3. Confirm Tulsa PS draft status (scheduled Tuesday 8:05 AM) — no action beyond noting whether it landed.

 4. Do not touch Pittsburgh PS (PA) or Archdiocese of Chicago (IL) queue rows — held for Steven's manual review.

 Phase 1 — Verify the memory's description of the Lackland row (< 10 min, read-only)

 Decision gate:

 - If memory is accurate (Strategy at col 3, Status at col 14, 11-value pattern): continue to Phase 2's "old layout hunt".

 - If memory is inaccurate (e.g., these rows are actually 19-element writes from a 19-element writer we haven't yet traced to F2): skip the ghost-writer hunt, jump straight to Phase 3 repair + Phase 5 writer

 fix for the 19-element bugs.

 Phase 2 — Scope the historical damage (< 30 min, read-only)

 Single Python script (no "harness," just a script) that reads every row from TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE via service account and classifies each by fingerprint. Output table: count by fingerprint × strategy × source

 × year-month.

 Fingerprints to detect:

 - F_canonical: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Signal ID at idx 18, Notes at idx 19

 - F_19col: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Notes at idx 18, idx 19 empty (the 19-element bug)

 - F_lackland: Strategy at idx 2, Priority at idx 3, Source at idx 4, Est Enrollment at idx 5, Notes at idx 6, Name Key at idx 12, Status at idx 13, Date at idx 14, Signal ID at idx 19

 - F_legacy16: Strategy at idx 5 (the target of the existing migration)

 - F_unknown: anything else

 Deliverable: row counts per fingerprint, oldest+newest date per fingerprint, strategies observed per fingerprint, 3 sample rows per fingerprint. If any F_unknown group has meaningful size, inspect a sample

 to classify it before moving on.

 This classifier is the same code the Phase 3 repair script uses — write it once, reuse.

 Phase 3 — Snapshot + repair script (dry-run first)

 1. Snapshot first. Before any write, copy TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE to a new tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 via a spreadsheets.sheets.copyTo API call (or a clear+update into a new tab). This is

 non-negotiable — without a backup, any repair bug is unrecoverable.

 2. Write scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py :

 - Uses the Phase 2 classifier to assign a fingerprint to every row

 - For each fingerprint, defines a deterministic index→index remap to canonical positions

 - Writes a dry-run report to stdout: "N rows of F_19col would be rewritten; 3 sample before/after" (per fingerprint)

 - Only after operator approval, does a clear+update of the tab using the migrate_prospect_columns pattern (lines 228–239) as reference — same atomic-ish pattern Scout already uses

 3. Remap rules:

 - F_19col → F_canonical: row[:18] + [""] + [row[18]]. (Same logic the existing migrator uses for the 19-col → 20-col case.)

 - F_lackland → F_canonical: rebuild from known positions: new[0]=old[0] , new[1]=old[1] , new[5]="" , new[6]="" , new[7]=old[12] , new[8]=old[2] , new[9]=old[4] , new[10]=old[13] , new[11]=old[3] ,

 new[12]=old[14] , new[15]=old[5] , new[18]=old[19] , new[19]=old[6] , all other positions = "" . Document these explicitly in the script as constants, not magic numbers scattered through code.

 - F_legacy16 → F_canonical: already handled by existing migrate_prospect_columns ; reuse its logic, don't reinvent.

 4. Dry-run-then-commit flow: The script must have two modes — --dry-run (default) prints diff only, --apply actually writes. No --apply without reviewing --dry-run output first.

 5. Failure mode: If the apply step crashes mid-write, the backup tab is the recovery path. Document the recovery command in the script's docstring.

 Phase 4 — Fix the writers

 Patch all five 19-element writers to include the Signal ID slot before Notes. One commit per file for surgical rollback:

 - tools/district_prospector.py : fix discover_districts:1141-1161 , suggest_upward_targets:1229-1249 , suggest_closed_lost_targets:1680-1700 , suggest_cold_license_requests:2327-2347 . Add "", #

 Signal ID at the correct position (one line above Notes) in each.

 - tools/proximity_engine.py : fix add_proximity_prospects:415-435 the same way.

 - Also fix _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 : change the read range from A:S to A:T and ensure the write preserves col 20. (Same commit as district_prospector fix.)

 - Extend migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES ( district_prospector.py:181 ) to include every Session 49+ strategy listed above. This is belt-and-suspenders protection against future legacy-row drift.

 If Phase 1 found the Lackland layout is real , additionally:

 - git log -p --all --diff-filter=D -- tools/district_prospector.py tools/signal_processor.py looking for deleted row-construction code that produces the 11-value pattern. If found, confirm it has no current

 callers (it shouldn't). If it does have callers, fix them.

 - Add stack-trace logging to _write_rows as a temporary diagnostic commit; deploy; force-run F2 with kill switch ON; capture evidence; revert the logging commit. Only do this if the Lackland fingerprint

 survives Phase 3 repair (i.e., new F_lackland rows appear post-repair).

 Phase 5 — Apply the repair

 1. Run repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py --dry-run against live sheet. Review stdout diff.

 2. Spot-check 3 specific rows manually in the diff: Lackland ISD, Kennedy Junior High (winback, 2026-03-20), one proximity row.

 3. If diff looks correct, run --apply . Sheet clears + rewrites.

 4. Immediately verify:

 - /prospect_all in Telegram returns Lackland ISD, Mansfield TX, Naperville 203 IL, and the other 5 F2 rows

 - /prospect_all count delta ≈ 1920 (the 8 F2 + ~1912 pre-existing that are now readable)

 - Backup tab is untouched and still readable

 Phase 6 — Re-enable scanners + sentinel test

 1. Deploy the Phase 4 commits (one per file).

 2. Flip ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN back to True . Leave F4/F5 off until their own plans in the sprint.

 3. Run F2 manually for one state (IL or TX). Wait for completion.

 4. Phase 2 classifier re-run: confirm any new rows fingerprint as F_canonical . If yes, BUG 3 is fixed. If no, the ghost writer is still out there and Phase 4's stack-trace diagnostic kicks in.

 Exit criteria (BUG 3 is DONE when all of these hold)

 1. Phase 2 classifier shows zero rows in any fingerprint other than F_canonical after repair

 2. /prospect_all returns the 8 F2 rows from tonight (Lackland, Mansfield, Naperville 203, etc.) with Status="pending" and correct Priority

 3. Manual F2 run in Phase 6 produces rows that fingerprint as F_canonical

 4. Backup tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 exists and is readable

 5. Pittsburgh PS and Archdiocese rows are still intact and untouched (double-check via direct read)

 6. scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py is committed and its docstring documents the recovery procedure

 7. All five writer fixes are committed, each in its own commit

 Critical files (reference only, not exhaustive change list)

 - tools/district_prospector.py — writers at 1141, 1229, 1461, 1680, 2327; infrastructure at 144 ( _ensure_tab ), 173 ( migrate_prospect_columns ), 426 ( _write_rows ), 440 ( _update_status ), 2482 ( add_district )

 - tools/proximity_engine.py:394-441 — add_proximity_prospects writer

 - tools/signal_processor.py:3748-3764 — F2 auto-queue block (calls add_district , verified canonical)

 - scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py — new file (Phase 3)

 - agent/main.py:2996 — /prospect_add dispatch (verified canonical)

 Out of scope for this plan

 BUGS 1, 2, 4, 5 (separate plans during the same sprint). Stages 6-8 Session 52 carryover (blocked on BUG 3 completion). Any new feature, scanner, or strategy.

 Questions before execution

 1. Snapshot method: copy to a new tab ( BACKUP_YYYYMMDD ) or download to a local JSON? Recommend the new tab — faster to restore from, no local file state.

 2. Railway evidence: do we have the Railway CLI or API access needed to pull deployment logs for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC in Phase 0? If not, Phase 0 step 2 drops to "best effort" and we rely on Phase 1

 verification.

 3. Stack-trace diagnostic deploy: acceptable to deploy a temporary debug-logging commit to Railway only if Phase 1 confirms Lackland layout is real AND Phase 4 archaeology finds no deleted writer? (Would

 revert the same day.)

 ---

 Changes from the prior version + why

 1. Discovered the real bug class before writing phases. Prior version delegated the "audit internal callers" to Phase 3. That was the entire investigation. Moving it into the planning pass itself cut five

 unknowns down to one (the Lackland mystery) and lets the plan lead with evidence instead of speculation.

 2. Dropped the Serper-replay harness. It belongs in the BUG 1 (F4 query redesign) plan, not here. BUG 3 doesn't need to replay Serper — it needs to read the queue, call add_district in isolation, and

 inspect results. That's a 20-line script, not a harness.

 3. Reordered phases to lead with a ground-truth check. Phase 1 in the new plan (re-read row 1947 to verify memory) is a 10-minute read-only sanity check that could collapse half the plan. Prior version had

 this buried inside Phase 4 "capture evidence".

 4. Made the snapshot a first-class phase step. Prior plan mentioned backups in passing. This one makes backing up to a new tab a hard prerequisite before any repair write, with a documented recovery

 command. That's how you avoid a "helpful" repair script destroying 1912 rows.

 5. Explicit dry-run-then-apply split. Repair script is --dry-run by default; --apply is gated on operator review of the dry-run diff. Prior plan said "dry run to stdout" but didn't make it a hard flag-gate.

 6. Kill switch state is explicit. Phase 0 flips F2 and F8 off before any investigation and Phase 6 flips F2 back on after verification. Prior plan said "consider flipping" — ambiguous.

 7. Exit criteria are quantified and measurable. Prior plan had "verification" bullet points that mixed "it works" with "we believe it works". New version is 7 specific, checkable conditions.

 8. Repair fingerprint math is spelled out as index remaps, not hand-waved. The prior plan gestured at "position substitution"; this one gives the explicit index→index mapping for each fingerprint. That's

 the part most likely to have a bug, so it needs to be reviewable before executing.

 9. Audit of _update_status and migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES folded in. Both were listed as "latent bugs found along the way" in the prior version. New version groups them into Phase 4 as part

 of the writer fix, because they touch the same file and shipping them separately is pointless churn.

 10. Added Lackland-mystery branch. Instead of treating the ghost writer as THE central problem, the plan now acknowledges it may be the memory mis-describing row 1947. Phase 1 collapses the whole branch in

 one read. If the memory was accurate, Phase 4's archaeology + stack-trace diagnostic kicks in as a contingency. If the memory was wrong, we avoid a multi-day rabbit hole.

 seed queue which already ran).

 Session 54 — Fix Sprint: BUG 3 (Queue Column Corruption) Investigation + Fix Plan

 Context

 Session 53's Fire Drill Audit uncovered 5 silent-failure bugs. BUG 3 is the highest-priority blocker: the Prospecting Queue contains rows written in non-canonical column layouts, so /prospect_all silently

 can't see them. 8 rows from tonight's F2 run + 1912 pre-existing rows (some dating to 2026-03-20) are affected. Running Stages 6-8 carryover without a fix would compound the damage. This plan investigates,

 repairs existing damage, and fixes the writer(s) responsible.

 Canonical layout (20 cols, from tools/district_prospector.py:PROSPECT_COLUMNS + add_district at 2482-2564):

 1 State | 2 Account Name | 3 Email | 4 First Name | 5 Last Name | 6 Deal Level | 7 Parent District |

 8 Name Key | 9 Strategy | 10 Source | 11 Status | 12 Priority | 13 Date Added | 14 Date Approved |

 15 Sequence Doc URL | 16 Est. Enrollment | 17 School Count | 18 Total Licenses | 19 Signal ID | 20 Notes

 What I found in this planning pass that wasn't in the memory

 Session 53 audited add_district and external callers but never audited the five internal _write_rows callers inside district_prospector.py . Four of them have the bug class that explains most of the

 historical damage. This cuts the investigation scope dramatically:

 ┌──────────────────────────────────┬───────────┬────────────┬─────────────────────────┐

 │ Function │ Line │ Row length │ Missing Signal ID slot? │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ discover_districts │ 1141–1161 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_upward_targets │ 1229–1249 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_intra_district_expansion │ 1461–1482 │ 20 │ ✅ ok │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_closed_lost_targets │ 1680–1700 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_cold_license_requests │ 2327–2347 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 └──────────────────────────────────┴───────────┴────────────┴─────────────────────────┘

 Plus proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415–435) also writes 19. That's five writers producing "canonical-minus-Signal-ID" rows for strategies cold , upward , winback ,

 cold_license_request , proximity . The Signal ID column was added in commit 33e34f6 (early April). These callers were never updated. This explains the Kennedy Junior High winback row from 2026-03-20 that the

 memory describes.

 Also found: _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 reads A:S (19 cols) and writes back via padded[:len(headers)] — it silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update. Separate latent bug in

 the same file.

 Also found: migrate_prospect_columns at district_prospector.py:173-251 has a _KNOWN_STRATEGIES set that only lists 8 strategies ( upward, cold, winback, cold_license_request, trigger, sequence_reengagement,

 webinar_attendee, webinar_missed ). Missing ALL Session 49+ strategies: competitor_displacement, csta_partnership, charter_cmo, cte_center, private_school_network, compliance_gap, intra_district,

 cs_funding_recipient, homeschool_coop, proximity, esa_cluster . The migrator therefore silently passes new-strategy scrambled rows through as-is. A secondary gap.

 The part that's still a mystery

 The memory describes tonight's 8 F2 rows as having an even more scrambled fingerprint than the 19-element writers would produce:

 col 1: TX ← state

 col 2: Lackland ISD ← name

 col 3: competitor_displacement ← strategy (should be col 9)

 col 4: 654 ← priority (should be col 12)

 col 5: signal ← source (should be col 10)

 col 6: 1035 ← enrollment (should be col 16)

 col 7: Competitor: ... ← notes (should be col 20)

 col 8-12: empty

 col 13: lackland ← name_key (should be col 8)

 col 14: pending ← status (should be col 11)

 col 15: 2026-04-10 00:51 ← date_added (should be col 13)

 col 16-19: empty

 col 20: competitor_lackland... ← signal_id (should be col 19)

 That's 11 filled cells in a specific pattern — not a 19-element append (which would fill cols 1–19 contiguously with Notes landing at col 19). No writer in the current repo produces this. Working hypotheses

 in order of likelihood:

 1. The memory misreads the row. The /prospect_all visibility failure could have a simpler explanation (e.g., Status is actually correct at col 11 but /prospect_all filters on Date Approved being empty, or a

 similar read-side bug). The fastest test is re-reading row 1947 directly.

 2. Stale Railway worker running a pre-Session-34 layout (State, Name, Strategy, Priority, Source, Enrollment, Notes…). Confirmable via Railway deployment logs for the 00:48–00:52 UTC window on 2026-04-10.

 3. A deleted code path still reachable. Deleted versions of _write_rows callers may have written rows in the old 11-value pattern. git log --all --diff-filter=D on the relevant files will surface them.

 Execution plan — tight phases, stop-and-verify between each

 Phase 0 — Preflight (< 15 min, read-only)

 1. Flip kill switches to False for the duration of investigation: ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN , ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY . ( ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN , ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN already False per commit 3431323 .) No new

 writes to the queue from any scheduled scanner until Phase 6 lands.

 2. Confirm Railway state: current singleton deployment is on commit 7c345a07 (or newer). Pull Railway deployment log for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC and check for rolling-deploy overlap. Record result in

 plan annotations.

 3. Confirm Tulsa PS draft status (scheduled Tuesday 8:05 AM) — no action beyond noting whether it landed.

 4. Do not touch Pittsburgh PS (PA) or Archdiocese of Chicago (IL) queue rows — held for Steven's manual review.

 Phase 1 — Verify the memory's description of the Lackland row (< 10 min, read-only)

 Decision gate:

 - If memory is accurate (Strategy at col 3, Status at col 14, 11-value pattern): continue to Phase 2's "old layout hunt".

 - If memory is inaccurate (e.g., these rows are actually 19-element writes from a 19-element writer we haven't yet traced to F2): skip the ghost-writer hunt, jump straight to Phase 3 repair + Phase 5 writer

 fix for the 19-element bugs.

 Phase 2 — Scope the historical damage (< 30 min, read-only)

 Single Python script (no "harness," just a script) that reads every row from TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE via service account and classifies each by fingerprint. Output table: count by fingerprint × strategy × source

 × year-month.

 Fingerprints to detect:

 - F_canonical: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Signal ID at idx 18, Notes at idx 19

 - F_19col: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Notes at idx 18, idx 19 empty (the 19-element bug)

 - F_lackland: Strategy at idx 2, Priority at idx 3, Source at idx 4, Est Enrollment at idx 5, Notes at idx 6, Name Key at idx 12, Status at idx 13, Date at idx 14, Signal ID at idx 19

 - F_legacy16: Strategy at idx 5 (the target of the existing migration)

 - F_unknown: anything else

 Deliverable: row counts per fingerprint, oldest+newest date per fingerprint, strategies observed per fingerprint, 3 sample rows per fingerprint. If any F_unknown group has meaningful size, inspect a sample

 to classify it before moving on.

 This classifier is the same code the Phase 3 repair script uses — write it once, reuse.

 Phase 3 — Snapshot + repair script (dry-run first)

 1. Snapshot first. Before any write, copy TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE to a new tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 via a spreadsheets.sheets.copyTo API call (or a clear+update into a new tab). This is

 non-negotiable — without a backup, any repair bug is unrecoverable.

 2. Write scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py :

 - Uses the Phase 2 classifier to assign a fingerprint to every row

 - For each fingerprint, defines a deterministic index→index remap to canonical positions

 - Writes a dry-run report to stdout: "N rows of F_19col would be rewritten; 3 sample before/after" (per fingerprint)

 - Only after operator approval, does a clear+update of the tab using the migrate_prospect_columns pattern (lines 228–239) as reference — same atomic-ish pattern Scout already uses

 3. Remap rules:

 - F_19col → F_canonical: row[:18] + [""] + [row[18]]. (Same logic the existing migrator uses for the 19-col → 20-col case.)

 - F_lackland → F_canonical: rebuild from known positions: new[0]=old[0] , new[1]=old[1] , new[5]="" , new[6]="" , new[7]=old[12] , new[8]=old[2] , new[9]=old[4] , new[10]=old[13] , new[11]=old[3] ,

 new[12]=old[14] , new[15]=old[5] , new[18]=old[19] , new[19]=old[6] , all other positions = "" . Document these explicitly in the script as constants, not magic numbers scattered through code.

 - F_legacy16 → F_canonical: already handled by existing migrate_prospect_columns ; reuse its logic, don't reinvent.

 4. Dry-run-then-commit flow: The script must have two modes — --dry-run (default) prints diff only, --apply actually writes. No --apply without reviewing --dry-run output first.

 5. Failure mode: If the apply step crashes mid-write, the backup tab is the recovery path. Document the recovery command in the script's docstring.

 Phase 4 — Fix the writers

 Patch all five 19-element writers to include the Signal ID slot before Notes. One commit per file for surgical rollback:

 - tools/district_prospector.py : fix discover_districts:1141-1161 , suggest_upward_targets:1229-1249 , suggest_closed_lost_targets:1680-1700 , suggest_cold_license_requests:2327-2347 . Add "", #

 Signal ID at the correct position (one line above Notes) in each.

 - tools/proximity_engine.py : fix add_proximity_prospects:415-435 the same way.

 - Also fix _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 : change the read range from A:S to A:T and ensure the write preserves col 20. (Same commit as district_prospector fix.)

 - Extend migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES ( district_prospector.py:181 ) to include every Session 49+ strategy listed above. This is belt-and-suspenders protection against future legacy-row drift.

 If Phase 1 found the Lackland layout is real , additionally:

 - git log -p --all --diff-filter=D -- tools/district_prospector.py tools/signal_processor.py looking for deleted row-construction code that produces the 11-value pattern. If found, confirm it has no current

 callers (it shouldn't). If it does have callers, fix them.

 - Add stack-trace logging to _write_rows as a temporary diagnostic commit; deploy; force-run F2 with kill switch ON; capture evidence; revert the logging commit. Only do this if the Lackland fingerprint

 survives Phase 3 repair (i.e., new F_lackland rows appear post-repair).

 Phase 5 — Apply the repair

 1. Run repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py --dry-run against live sheet. Review stdout diff.

 2. Spot-check 3 specific rows manually in the diff: Lackland ISD, Kennedy Junior High (winback, 2026-03-20), one proximity row.

 3. If diff looks correct, run --apply . Sheet clears + rewrites.

 4. Immediately verify:

 - /prospect_all in Telegram returns Lackland ISD, Mansfield TX, Naperville 203 IL, and the other 5 F2 rows

 - /prospect_all count delta ≈ 1920 (the 8 F2 + ~1912 pre-existing that are now readable)

 - Backup tab is untouched and still readable

 Phase 6 — Re-enable scanners + sentinel test

 1. Deploy the Phase 4 commits (one per file).

 2. Flip ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN back to True . Leave F4/F5 off until their own plans in the sprint.

 3. Run F2 manually for one state (IL or TX). Wait for completion.

 4. Phase 2 classifier re-run: confirm any new rows fingerprint as F_canonical . If yes, BUG 3 is fixed. If no, the ghost writer is still out there and Phase 4's stack-trace diagnostic kicks in.

 Exit criteria (BUG 3 is DONE when all of these hold)

 1. Phase 2 classifier shows zero rows in any fingerprint other than F_canonical after repair

 2. /prospect_all returns the 8 F2 rows from tonight (Lackland, Mansfield, Naperville 203, etc.) with Status="pending" and correct Priority

 3. Manual F2 run in Phase 6 produces rows that fingerprint as F_canonical

 4. Backup tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 exists and is readable

 5. Pittsburgh PS and Archdiocese rows are still intact and untouched (double-check via direct read)

 6. scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py is committed and its docstring documents the recovery procedure

 7. All five writer fixes are committed, each in its own commit

 Critical files (reference only, not exhaustive change list)

 - tools/district_prospector.py — writers at 1141, 1229, 1461, 1680, 2327; infrastructure at 144 ( _ensure_tab ), 173 ( migrate_prospect_columns ), 426 ( _write_rows ), 440 ( _update_status ), 2482 ( add_district )

 - tools/proximity_engine.py:394-441 — add_proximity_prospects writer

 - tools/signal_processor.py:3748-3764 — F2 auto-queue block (calls add_district , verified canonical)

 - scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py — new file (Phase 3)

 - agent/main.py:2996 — /prospect_add dispatch (verified canonical)

 Out of scope for this plan

 BUGS 1, 2, 4, 5 (separate plans during the same sprint). Stages 6-8 Session 52 carryover (blocked on BUG 3 completion). Any new feature, scanner, or strategy.

 Questions before execution

 1. Snapshot method: copy to a new tab ( BACKUP_YYYYMMDD ) or download to a local JSON? Recommend the new tab — faster to restore from, no local file state.

 2. Railway evidence: do we have the Railway CLI or API access needed to pull deployment logs for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC in Phase 0? If not, Phase 0 step 2 drops to "best effort" and we rely on Phase 1

 verification.

 3. Stack-trace diagnostic deploy: acceptable to deploy a temporary debug-logging commit to Railway only if Phase 1 confirms Lackland layout is real AND Phase 4 archaeology finds no deleted writer? (Would

 revert the same day.)

 ---

 Changes from the prior version + why

 1. Discovered the real bug class before writing phases. Prior version delegated the "audit internal callers" to Phase 3. That was the entire investigation. Moving it into the planning pass itself cut five

 unknowns down to one (the Lackland mystery) and lets the plan lead with evidence instead of speculation.

 2. Dropped the Serper-replay harness. It belongs in the BUG 1 (F4 query redesign) plan, not here. BUG 3 doesn't need to replay Serper — it needs to read the queue, call add_district in isolation, and

 inspect results. That's a 20-line script, not a harness.

 3. Reordered phases to lead with a ground-truth check. Phase 1 in the new plan (re-read row 1947 to verify memory) is a 10-minute read-only sanity check that could collapse half the plan. Prior version had

 this buried inside Phase 4 "capture evidence".

 4. Made the snapshot a first-class phase step. Prior plan mentioned backups in passing. This one makes backing up to a new tab a hard prerequisite before any repair write, with a documented recovery

 command. That's how you avoid a "helpful" repair script destroying 1912 rows.

 5. Explicit dry-run-then-apply split. Repair script is --dry-run by default; --apply is gated on operator review of the dry-run diff. Prior plan said "dry run to stdout" but didn't make it a hard flag-gate.

 6. Kill switch state is explicit. Phase 0 flips F2 and F8 off before any investigation and Phase 6 flips F2 back on after verification. Prior plan said "consider flipping" — ambiguous.

 7. Exit criteria are quantified and measurable. Prior plan had "verification" bullet points that mixed "it works" with "we believe it works". New version is 7 specific, checkable conditions.

 8. Repair fingerprint math is spelled out as index remaps, not hand-waved. The prior plan gestured at "position substitution"; this one gives the explicit index→index mapping for each fingerprint. That's

 the part most likely to have a bug, so it needs to be reviewable before executing.

 9. Audit of _update_status and migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES folded in. Both were listed as "latent bugs found along the way" in the prior version. New version groups them into Phase 4 as part

 of the writer fix, because they touch the same file and shipping them separately is pointless churn.

 10. Added Lackland-mystery branch. Instead of treating the ghost writer as THE central problem, the plan now acknowledges it may be the memory mis-describing row 1947. Phase 1 collapses the whole branch in

 one read. If the memory was accurate, Phase 4's archaeology + stack-trace diagnostic kicks in as a contingency. If the memory was wrong, we avoid a multi-day rabbit hole.

 seed queue which already ran).

 ⏺ Key findings before exiting plan mode:

 - Agent 1's proximity bug is real but separate. proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415-435) writes 19 cols, missing Signal ID. That's a latent off-by-one, but its layout

 (Strategy at col 9) does NOT match the Lackland fingerprint (Strategy at col 3). Not the BUG 3 root cause — but fixing it is in Phase 6.

 - Agent 2's "F8 missing kwargs" theory is backwards. F8 Archdiocese was confirmed canonical by direct sheet read in Session 53; F2 Lackland was the scrambled one. Agent 2 misread the attribution. Ruled out.

 - _update_status (district_prospector.py:440-471) reads A:S (19 cols) and silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update — another latent bug. In Phase 6.

 migration runs.

 systematically.

 Session 54 — Fix Sprint: BUG 3 (Queue Column Corruption) Investigation + Fix Plan

 Context

 Session 53's Fire Drill Audit uncovered 5 silent-failure bugs. BUG 3 is the highest-priority blocker: the Prospecting Queue contains rows written in non-canonical column layouts, so /prospect_all silently

 can't see them. 8 rows from tonight's F2 run + 1912 pre-existing rows (some dating to 2026-03-20) are affected. Running Stages 6-8 carryover without a fix would compound the damage. This plan investigates,

 repairs existing damage, and fixes the writer(s) responsible.

 Canonical layout (20 cols, from tools/district_prospector.py:PROSPECT_COLUMNS + add_district at 2482-2564):

 1 State | 2 Account Name | 3 Email | 4 First Name | 5 Last Name | 6 Deal Level | 7 Parent District |

 8 Name Key | 9 Strategy | 10 Source | 11 Status | 12 Priority | 13 Date Added | 14 Date Approved |

 15 Sequence Doc URL | 16 Est. Enrollment | 17 School Count | 18 Total Licenses | 19 Signal ID | 20 Notes

 What I found in this planning pass that wasn't in the memory

 Session 53 audited add_district and external callers but never audited the five internal _write_rows callers inside district_prospector.py . Four of them have the bug class that explains most of the

 historical damage. This cuts the investigation scope dramatically:

 ┌──────────────────────────────────┬───────────┬────────────┬─────────────────────────┐

 │ Function │ Line │ Row length │ Missing Signal ID slot? │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ discover_districts │ 1141–1161 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_upward_targets │ 1229–1249 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_intra_district_expansion │ 1461–1482 │ 20 │ ✅ ok │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_closed_lost_targets │ 1680–1700 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 ├──────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼─────────────────────────┤

 │ suggest_cold_license_requests │ 2327–2347 │ 19 │ ❌ yes │

 └──────────────────────────────────┴───────────┴────────────┴─────────────────────────┘

 Plus proximity_engine.add_proximity_prospects (tools/proximity_engine.py:415–435) also writes 19. That's five writers producing "canonical-minus-Signal-ID" rows for strategies cold , upward , winback ,

 cold_license_request , proximity . The Signal ID column was added in commit 33e34f6 (early April). These callers were never updated. This explains the Kennedy Junior High winback row from 2026-03-20 that the

 memory describes.

 Also found: _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 reads A:S (19 cols) and writes back via padded[:len(headers)] — it silently destroys col 20 Notes on every status update. Separate latent bug in

 the same file.

 Also found: migrate_prospect_columns at district_prospector.py:173-251 has a _KNOWN_STRATEGIES set that only lists 8 strategies ( upward, cold, winback, cold_license_request, trigger, sequence_reengagement,

 webinar_attendee, webinar_missed ). Missing ALL Session 49+ strategies: competitor_displacement, csta_partnership, charter_cmo, cte_center, private_school_network, compliance_gap, intra_district,

 cs_funding_recipient, homeschool_coop, proximity, esa_cluster . The migrator therefore silently passes new-strategy scrambled rows through as-is. A secondary gap.

 The part that's still a mystery

 The memory describes tonight's 8 F2 rows as having an even more scrambled fingerprint than the 19-element writers would produce:

 col 1: TX ← state

 col 2: Lackland ISD ← name

 col 3: competitor_displacement ← strategy (should be col 9)

 col 4: 654 ← priority (should be col 12)

 col 5: signal ← source (should be col 10)

 col 6: 1035 ← enrollment (should be col 16)

 col 7: Competitor: ... ← notes (should be col 20)

 col 8-12: empty

 col 13: lackland ← name_key (should be col 8)

 col 14: pending ← status (should be col 11)

 col 15: 2026-04-10 00:51 ← date_added (should be col 13)

 col 16-19: empty

 col 20: competitor_lackland... ← signal_id (should be col 19)

 That's 11 filled cells in a specific pattern — not a 19-element append (which would fill cols 1–19 contiguously with Notes landing at col 19). No writer in the current repo produces this. Working hypotheses

 in order of likelihood:

 1. The memory misreads the row. The /prospect_all visibility failure could have a simpler explanation (e.g., Status is actually correct at col 11 but /prospect_all filters on Date Approved being empty, or a

 similar read-side bug). The fastest test is re-reading row 1947 directly.

 2. Stale Railway worker running a pre-Session-34 layout (State, Name, Strategy, Priority, Source, Enrollment, Notes…). Confirmable via Railway deployment logs for the 00:48–00:52 UTC window on 2026-04-10.

 3. A deleted code path still reachable. Deleted versions of _write_rows callers may have written rows in the old 11-value pattern. git log --all --diff-filter=D on the relevant files will surface them.

 Execution plan — tight phases, stop-and-verify between each

 Phase 0 — Preflight (< 15 min, read-only)

 1. Flip kill switches to False for the duration of investigation: ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN , ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY . ( ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN , ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN already False per commit 3431323 .) No new

 writes to the queue from any scheduled scanner until Phase 6 lands.

 2. Confirm Railway state: current singleton deployment is on commit 7c345a07 (or newer). Pull Railway deployment log for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC and check for rolling-deploy overlap. Record result in

 plan annotations.

 3. Confirm Tulsa PS draft status (scheduled Tuesday 8:05 AM) — no action beyond noting whether it landed.

 4. Do not touch Pittsburgh PS (PA) or Archdiocese of Chicago (IL) queue rows — held for Steven's manual review.

 Phase 1 — Verify the memory's description of the Lackland row (< 10 min, read-only)

 Decision gate:

 - If memory is accurate (Strategy at col 3, Status at col 14, 11-value pattern): continue to Phase 2's "old layout hunt".

 - If memory is inaccurate (e.g., these rows are actually 19-element writes from a 19-element writer we haven't yet traced to F2): skip the ghost-writer hunt, jump straight to Phase 3 repair + Phase 5 writer

 fix for the 19-element bugs.

 Phase 2 — Scope the historical damage (< 30 min, read-only)

 Single Python script (no "harness," just a script) that reads every row from TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE via service account and classifies each by fingerprint. Output table: count by fingerprint × strategy × source

 × year-month.

 Fingerprints to detect:

 - F_canonical: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Signal ID at idx 18, Notes at idx 19

 - F_19col: Strategy at idx 8, Status at idx 10, Notes at idx 18, idx 19 empty (the 19-element bug)

 - F_lackland: Strategy at idx 2, Priority at idx 3, Source at idx 4, Est Enrollment at idx 5, Notes at idx 6, Name Key at idx 12, Status at idx 13, Date at idx 14, Signal ID at idx 19

 - F_legacy16: Strategy at idx 5 (the target of the existing migration)

 - F_unknown: anything else

 Deliverable: row counts per fingerprint, oldest+newest date per fingerprint, strategies observed per fingerprint, 3 sample rows per fingerprint. If any F_unknown group has meaningful size, inspect a sample

 to classify it before moving on.

 This classifier is the same code the Phase 3 repair script uses — write it once, reuse.

 Phase 3 — Snapshot + repair script (dry-run first)

 1. Snapshot first. Before any write, copy TAB_PROSPECT_QUEUE to a new tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 via a spreadsheets.sheets.copyTo API call (or a clear+update into a new tab). This is

 non-negotiable — without a backup, any repair bug is unrecoverable.

 2. Write scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py :

 - Uses the Phase 2 classifier to assign a fingerprint to every row

 - For each fingerprint, defines a deterministic index→index remap to canonical positions

 - Writes a dry-run report to stdout: "N rows of F_19col would be rewritten; 3 sample before/after" (per fingerprint)

 - Only after operator approval, does a clear+update of the tab using the migrate_prospect_columns pattern (lines 228–239) as reference — same atomic-ish pattern Scout already uses

 3. Remap rules:

 - F_19col → F_canonical: row[:18] + [""] + [row[18]]. (Same logic the existing migrator uses for the 19-col → 20-col case.)

 - F_lackland → F_canonical: rebuild from known positions: new[0]=old[0] , new[1]=old[1] , new[5]="" , new[6]="" , new[7]=old[12] , new[8]=old[2] , new[9]=old[4] , new[10]=old[13] , new[11]=old[3] ,

 new[12]=old[14] , new[15]=old[5] , new[18]=old[19] , new[19]=old[6] , all other positions = "" . Document these explicitly in the script as constants, not magic numbers scattered through code.

 - F_legacy16 → F_canonical: already handled by existing migrate_prospect_columns ; reuse its logic, don't reinvent.

 4. Dry-run-then-commit flow: The script must have two modes — --dry-run (default) prints diff only, --apply actually writes. No --apply without reviewing --dry-run output first.

 5. Failure mode: If the apply step crashes mid-write, the backup tab is the recovery path. Document the recovery command in the script's docstring.

 Phase 4 — Fix the writers

 Patch all five 19-element writers to include the Signal ID slot before Notes. One commit per file for surgical rollback:

 - tools/district_prospector.py : fix discover_districts:1141-1161 , suggest_upward_targets:1229-1249 , suggest_closed_lost_targets:1680-1700 , suggest_cold_license_requests:2327-2347 . Add "", #

 Signal ID at the correct position (one line above Notes) in each.

 - tools/proximity_engine.py : fix add_proximity_prospects:415-435 the same way.

 - Also fix _update_status at district_prospector.py:440-471 : change the read range from A:S to A:T and ensure the write preserves col 20. (Same commit as district_prospector fix.)

 - Extend migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES ( district_prospector.py:181 ) to include every Session 49+ strategy listed above. This is belt-and-suspenders protection against future legacy-row drift.

 If Phase 1 found the Lackland layout is real , additionally:

 - git log -p --all --diff-filter=D -- tools/district_prospector.py tools/signal_processor.py looking for deleted row-construction code that produces the 11-value pattern. If found, confirm it has no current

 callers (it shouldn't). If it does have callers, fix them.

 - Add stack-trace logging to _write_rows as a temporary diagnostic commit; deploy; force-run F2 with kill switch ON; capture evidence; revert the logging commit. Only do this if the Lackland fingerprint

 survives Phase 3 repair (i.e., new F_lackland rows appear post-repair).

 Phase 5 — Apply the repair

 1. Run repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py --dry-run against live sheet. Review stdout diff.

 2. Spot-check 3 specific rows manually in the diff: Lackland ISD, Kennedy Junior High (winback, 2026-03-20), one proximity row.

 3. If diff looks correct, run --apply . Sheet clears + rewrites.

 4. Immediately verify:

 - /prospect_all in Telegram returns Lackland ISD, Mansfield TX, Naperville 203 IL, and the other 5 F2 rows

 - /prospect_all count delta ≈ 1920 (the 8 F2 + ~1912 pre-existing that are now readable)

 - Backup tab is untouched and still readable

 Phase 6 — Re-enable scanners + sentinel test

 1. Deploy the Phase 4 commits (one per file).

 2. Flip ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN back to True . Leave F4/F5 off until their own plans in the sprint.

 3. Run F2 manually for one state (IL or TX). Wait for completion.

 4. Phase 2 classifier re-run: confirm any new rows fingerprint as F_canonical . If yes, BUG 3 is fixed. If no, the ghost writer is still out there and Phase 4's stack-trace diagnostic kicks in.

 Exit criteria (BUG 3 is DONE when all of these hold)

 1. Phase 2 classifier shows zero rows in any fingerprint other than F_canonical after repair

 2. /prospect_all returns the 8 F2 rows from tonight (Lackland, Mansfield, Naperville 203, etc.) with Status="pending" and correct Priority

 3. Manual F2 run in Phase 6 produces rows that fingerprint as F_canonical

 4. Backup tab Prospecting Queue BACKUP 2026-04-10 exists and is readable

 5. Pittsburgh PS and Archdiocese rows are still intact and untouched (double-check via direct read)

 6. scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py is committed and its docstring documents the recovery procedure

 7. All five writer fixes are committed, each in its own commit

 Critical files (reference only, not exhaustive change list)

 - tools/district_prospector.py — writers at 1141, 1229, 1461, 1680, 2327; infrastructure at 144 ( _ensure_tab ), 173 ( migrate_prospect_columns ), 426 ( _write_rows ), 440 ( _update_status ), 2482 ( add_district )

 - tools/proximity_engine.py:394-441 — add_proximity_prospects writer

 - tools/signal_processor.py:3748-3764 — F2 auto-queue block (calls add_district , verified canonical)

 - scripts/repair_scrambled_queue_rows.py — new file (Phase 3)

 - agent/main.py:2996 — /prospect_add dispatch (verified canonical)

 Out of scope for this plan

 BUGS 1, 2, 4, 5 (separate plans during the same sprint). Stages 6-8 Session 52 carryover (blocked on BUG 3 completion). Any new feature, scanner, or strategy.

 Questions before execution

 1. Snapshot method: copy to a new tab ( BACKUP_YYYYMMDD ) or download to a local JSON? Recommend the new tab — faster to restore from, no local file state.

 2. Railway evidence: do we have the Railway CLI or API access needed to pull deployment logs for 2026-04-10 00:48–00:52 UTC in Phase 0? If not, Phase 0 step 2 drops to "best effort" and we rely on Phase 1

 verification.

 3. Stack-trace diagnostic deploy: acceptable to deploy a temporary debug-logging commit to Railway only if Phase 1 confirms Lackland layout is real AND Phase 4 archaeology finds no deleted writer? (Would

 revert the same day.)

 ---

 Changes from the prior version + why

 1. Discovered the real bug class before writing phases. Prior version delegated the "audit internal callers" to Phase 3. That was the entire investigation. Moving it into the planning pass itself cut five

 unknowns down to one (the Lackland mystery) and lets the plan lead with evidence instead of speculation.

 2. Dropped the Serper-replay harness. It belongs in the BUG 1 (F4 query redesign) plan, not here. BUG 3 doesn't need to replay Serper — it needs to read the queue, call add_district in isolation, and

 inspect results. That's a 20-line script, not a harness.

 3. Reordered phases to lead with a ground-truth check. Phase 1 in the new plan (re-read row 1947 to verify memory) is a 10-minute read-only sanity check that could collapse half the plan. Prior version had

 this buried inside Phase 4 "capture evidence".

 4. Made the snapshot a first-class phase step. Prior plan mentioned backups in passing. This one makes backing up to a new tab a hard prerequisite before any repair write, with a documented recovery

 command. That's how you avoid a "helpful" repair script destroying 1912 rows.

 5. Explicit dry-run-then-apply split. Repair script is --dry-run by default; --apply is gated on operator review of the dry-run diff. Prior plan said "dry run to stdout" but didn't make it a hard flag-gate.

 6. Kill switch state is explicit. Phase 0 flips F2 and F8 off before any investigation and Phase 6 flips F2 back on after verification. Prior plan said "consider flipping" — ambiguous.

 7. Exit criteria are quantified and measurable. Prior plan had "verification" bullet points that mixed "it works" with "we believe it works". New version is 7 specific, checkable conditions.

 8. Repair fingerprint math is spelled out as index remaps, not hand-waved. The prior plan gestured at "position substitution"; this one gives the explicit index→index mapping for each fingerprint. That's

 the part most likely to have a bug, so it needs to be reviewable before executing.

 9. Audit of _update_status and migrate_prospect_columns._KNOWN_STRATEGIES folded in. Both were listed as "latent bugs found along the way" in the prior version. New version groups them into Phase 4 as part

 of the writer fix, because they touch the same file and shipping them separately is pointless churn.

 10. Added Lackland-mystery branch. Instead of treating the ghost writer as THE central problem, the plan now acknowledges it may be the memory mis-describing row 1947. Phase 1 collapses the whole branch in

 one read. If the memory was accurate, Phase 4's archaeology + stack-trace diagnostic kicks in as a contingency. If the memory was wrong, we avoid a multi-day rabbit hole.

 seed queue which already ran).
